## Agent 6 continued attack — reducing the moving wall to an ODE-rigidity obstruction

We now assume the \(Y12\) quotient survives and that the honest wall is the moving line

\[
\ell
=
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2},
\qquad \alpha_9\neq0.
\]

The prior conclusion was that raw D4 anisotropy is insufficient; the wall theorem must control the actual visible normal component. The useful reduction is that failure of wall anti-concentration forces a rigid fourth-order differential relation for the canonical phase. 

---

# 1. Normalize the wall

Set

\[
A=\Gamma_G,
\qquad
B=\Gamma_{G,2},
\qquad
r=\frac{\beta_9}{\alpha_9}.
\]

Then, after dyadic freezing of \(\alpha_9\),

\[
\ell\asymp_Q B+rA.
\]

The wall anti-concentration theorem is

\[
\nu(|B+rA|<\varepsilon)
\le Q^C\varepsilon^\theta\nu(\mathcal C).
\]

The only way this can fail badly is if

\[
B+rA
\]

has high-order flatness or persistent vanishing on the routed mass.

---

# 2. The wall equation is fourth-order

Recall

\[
A=\Gamma_G=u-6q_0+2q_0^3,
\]

\[
B=\Gamma_{G,2}=(1-q_0^2)u-\frac16q^{(4)},
\]

with

\[
u=q''_{\can}.
\]

Then

\[
B+rA=0
\]

is equivalent to

\[
(1-q_0^2)u-\frac16q^{(4)}
+r(u-6q_0+2q_0^3)=0.
\]

So

\[
\boxed{
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+r)u
-36rq_0
+12rq_0^3.
}
\]

This is the **D4 wall ODE**.

Thus, persistent wall failure is not an arbitrary cancellation. It says the canonical phase satisfies a fourth-order algebraic differential constraint whose coefficient \(r\) is dictated by the local D4 carrier algebra.

---

# 3. Finite-type theorem to prove

The wall theorem can be reduced to:

\[
\boxed{
B+rA
\text{ has polynomial finite type under the post-Gate/O2O3 measure.}
}
\]

More explicitly, on every routed interval \(I\) with non-negligible mass, there should exist

\[
0\le j\le J
\]

such that

\[
\left|
\frac{d^j}{dm^j}(B+rA)
\right|
\ge Q^{-C}
\]

on a controlled subinterval or in a standard finite-type norm.

Then van der Corput / one-dimensional sublevel estimates give

\[
\nu(|B+rA|<\varepsilon)
\le Q^C\varepsilon^{1/J}\nu(I).
\]

So the required theorem is not a pointwise lower bound. It is a finite-order non-flatness theorem for the D4 wall equation.

---

# 4. Ratio form on the \(|A|\)-large set

On

\[
|A|\ge Q^{-C},
\]

write

\[
R_\Gamma=\frac{B}{A}.
\]

Then

\[
B+rA=A(R_\Gamma+r).
\]

The first derivative obstruction is

\[
W_\ell
=
AB'-A'B+r'A^2.
\]

Indeed,

\[
A^2(R_\Gamma+r)'=W_\ell.
\]

So, on \(|A|\)-large regions, a sufficient theorem is

\[
\boxed{
R_\Gamma+r
\text{ has polynomial finite type.}
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
W_\ell
\text{ and its derivatives do not vanish to high order on most mass.}
}
\]

This is the moving-wall analogue of a source-determinant non-flatness theorem.

---

# 5. Small-\(A\) region

On

\[
|A|<Q^{-C},
\]

the ratio \(B/A\) is not useful. There we need joint sublevel control:

\[
\boxed{
\nu(|A|<Q^{-C},\ |B|<\varepsilon)
\le Q^C\varepsilon^\theta\nu(\mathcal C).
}
\]

This is a two-component D4-wall nondegeneracy theorem:

\[
(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\]

cannot spend too much mass near \((0,0)\).

If this joint sublevel theorem fails, then the source is near both the order-seven and order-nine anisotropy walls. That is stronger than failure of the rank-one wall and should probably trigger a separate Gate–2/D4 degeneracy branch.

---

# 6. What finite-type failure implies

Suppose

\[
B+rA
\]

vanishes to very high order at \(m_0\). Then the derivatives of

\[
q^{(4)}-6(1-q_0^2+r)q''+36rq_0-12rq_0^3
\]

also vanish to high order.

This recursively forces higher derivatives

\[
q^{(5)},q^{(6)},\ldots
\]

to be controlled by lower jets and the finite D4 coefficient \(r\).

Thus high-order wall flatness implies the local canonical phase lies close to a finite-dimensional solution manifold of the D4 wall ODE.

So the finite-type theorem can be rephrased as:

\[
\boxed{
q_{\can}
\text{ is not locally trapped near the finite-dimensional D4-wall ODE solution family on polynomial mass.}
}
\]

This is another model-space exclusion theorem, parallel to:

\[
q''_{\can}\notin \text{branch holes}
\]

and

\[
D_{-n}\notin \operatorname{span}\{D_{-n+1},\ldots,D_{-n+d_0}\}.
\]

---

# 7. Possible proof route via actual EF source rigidity

Since

\[
q''_{\can}=B_2+P_2+D_2+E_2,
\]

the D4 wall ODE is an equation involving

\[
q'',\quad q^{(4)},\quad q_0,
\quad r(q_0,q'',\ldots).
\]

In the finite approximation,

\[
F_X=B_2+P_{2,\le X}+D_{2,\le X},
\]

this becomes a linear/nonlinear constraint on the same finite EF packet and its second derivative.

The wall ODE can be tested by applying the differential operator

\[
\mathcal L_r[F]
:=
F''-6(1-q_0^2+r)F
+36rq_0
-12rq_0^3,
\]

schematically, since \(F=q''\) and \(F''=q^{(4)}\).

The theorem needed is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal L_r(F_X)
\text{ is not small on polynomial mass unless }F_X\text{ is small or degenerate.}
}
\]

This is an \(L^2\) anti-lock theorem for a differential transform of the finite EF packet.

That is promising because it is closer to standard Dirichlet-polynomial mean-square technology than a raw pointwise wall estimate.

---

# 8. Relation to the \(Y12\) quotient

The \(Y12\) quotient determines \(r\). Once \(r\) is fixed by the quotient, the wall anti-concentration becomes a theorem about whether actual source geometry can align with that quotient-defined wall.

So the logical order is:

\[
Y12\text{ survival}
\Rightarrow
r=\beta_9/\alpha_9
\Rightarrow
\mathrm{MovingWallFiniteType}(r).
\]

Do not attempt to prove wall anti-concentration before \(r\) is known. The wrong wall line gives the wrong ODE.

---

# 9. What would make \(\beta_9\)-removal valuable

If a refined quotient proves

\[
\beta_9=0
\]

or kills the \(\Gamma_G\)-part, then

\[
B+rA
\]

reduces to

\[
B=\Gamma_{G,2}.
\]

Then the wall ODE becomes simply

\[
q^{(4)}=6(1-q_0^2)u.
\]

That is cleaner. But unless this is finite-audited, use the moving wall.

The project should not wait for \(\beta_9\)-removal. The moving wall is already a finite-type problem.

---

# 10. Updated wall theorem

The wall input should be named:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4WallFiniteType}(r).
}
\]

It consists of:

1. dyadic control of \(\alpha_9,\beta_9,r\);
2. finite-type sublevel control for \(R_\Gamma+r\) on \(|\Gamma_G|\)-large regions;
3. joint sublevel control for \((\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})\) on \(|\Gamma_G|\)-small regions;
4. exclusion of high-order trapping near the D4 wall ODE.

Then

\[
\int
\min(Q^A|\ell_m(\Gamma)|^2,1)\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-B}\nu(\mathcal C).
\]

---

# 11. Current best next attack

The next finite calculation should be:

\[
\boxed{
\text{compute or characterize }r=\beta_9/\alpha_9
\text{ from the }Y12\text{ quotient.}
}
\]

Then test whether

\[
r
\]

is a function only of low Gram variables \((q_0,u)\), or whether it also involves higher jets. If \(r\) is low-order, the D4 wall ODE is a genuine fourth-order constraint and is likely finite type. If \(r\) secretly contains \(q^{(4)}\)-like information, it may track the actual wall and cause degeneracy.

So:

\[
\boxed{
r\text{ must not depend on }q^{(4)}\text{ in a way that tautologically cancels }\Gamma_{G,2}.
}
\]

This is the next honest-wall audit.

---

# 12. Final status

The moving wall is not a vague obstacle. It is the finite-type exclusion of the D4 wall ODE:

\[
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+r)q''
-36rq_0
+12rq_0^3.
\]

If the actual source cannot satisfy this ODE to high order on polynomial mass, then the rank-one \(Y12\) wall has enough mass for extraction.

That is the cleanest current formulation of the wall anti-concentration problem.

## Agent 6 continued attack — auditing \(r=\beta_9/\alpha_9\)

The moving-wall theorem is now reduced to finite type for

\[
f=\Gamma_{G,2}+r\Gamma_G,
\qquad
r=\beta_9/\alpha_9.
\]

The decisive audit is whether \(r\) is a **low-order D4 carrier coefficient** or whether it secretly depends on the same fourth-order information contained in \(\Gamma_{G,2}\). If \(r\) is low-order, wall vanishing imposes a genuine fourth-order ODE and should be finite-type. If \(r\) contains \(q^{(4)}\)-scale information, the wall may be tautological.

---

# 1. What \(r\) is allowed to depend on

The clean D4 matrix is

\[
L_{\clean}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Here

\[
c_7
=
-\frac{1}
{24\pi^2gh(\sqrt g+\sqrt h)^2},
\]

and

\[
\alpha_9
=
\frac{1}
{64\pi^2\sqrt{gh}\,h^{3/2}(\sqrt g+\sqrt h)}.
\]

These are functions of the centered Gram diagonal scalars

\[
g=g_{\rm Gr},
\qquad
h=h_{\rm Gr},
\]

with

\[
g_{\rm Gr}=\frac{q_0}{\pi},
\qquad
h_{\rm Gr}
=
\frac1\pi\left(\frac13q_0^3+\frac16u\right),
\qquad
u=q''_{\can}.
\]

So \(c_7\) and \(\alpha_9\) depend only on

\[
q_0,\quad q''_{\can},
\]

through \(g,h\).

The audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\beta_9=\beta_9(g,h,u,\text{low whitening data})?
}
\]

or

\[
\boxed{
\beta_9\text{ contains }q^{(4)}\text{ or }\Gamma_{G,2}\text{ information?}
}
\]

If \(\beta_9\) only depends on low Gram/whitening variables, then

\[
r=\beta_9/\alpha_9
\]

is low-order. Good.

If \(\beta_9\) contains \(q^{(4)}\) or an order-nine connection coefficient extracted from the same source that defines \(\Gamma_{G,2}\), then the wall can become tautological. Bad.

---

# 2. Why \(r\) should be low-order in the current D4 algebra

The D4 clean matrix is a carrier conversion matrix. The entry

\[
\beta_9
\]

is the same-scale leakage of \(\Gamma_G\) into the order-nine carrier. It is part of the effective leading matrix, not a residual. Structurally, that leakage should be determined by the finite Gram/whitening geometry, not by the actual fourth derivative \(q^{(4)}\).

In the D4 formulation,

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}
=
(1-q_0^2)u-\frac16q^{(4)}
\]

is the second anisotropy variable. It is the **input** that \(\alpha_9\) multiplies.

If \(\beta_9\) also depended on \(q^{(4)}\), then the supposed linear transformation

\[
(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\mapsto
(B_7,B_9)
\]

would not really be a linear carrier matrix in the two anisotropy variables. It would hide part of one input inside a coefficient.

That would break the clean triangular interpretation.

So the expected structural statement is:

\[
\boxed{
\beta_9\text{ is a low-order coefficient depending on }g,h\text{ and fixed whitening algebra, not on }q^{(4)}.
}
\]

If this is true, then

\[
r=r(q_0,u)
\]

up to controlled chart/whitening terms.

---

# 3. Consequence: wall ODE is genuinely fourth-order

If

\[
r=r(q_0,u)
\]

with \(u=q''_{\can}\), then wall vanishing

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+r\Gamma_G=0
\]

gives

\[
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+r(q_0,u))u
-
36r(q_0,u)q_0
+
12r(q_0,u)q_0^3.
\]

This is a fourth-order ODE of the form

\[
q^{(4)}=F(q,q'?,q'',\ldots)
\]

more precisely involving \(q_0\) and \(q''\), with known low-order coefficient \(r\).

That is a real finite-type constraint. A generic actual EF phase cannot satisfy it on large mass unless forced by the zeta equation.

So the moving-wall anti-concentration reduces to:

\[
\boxed{
q_{\can}\text{ is not trapped near the low-order D4-wall ODE.}
}
\]

This is plausible.

---

# 4. If \(r\) depends on \(q^{(4)}\), the wall may be tautological

Suppose instead

\[
r
=
r_0(q_0,u)+r_1(q_0,u)q^{(4)}
\]

or depends directly on \(\Gamma_{G,2}\). Then

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+r\Gamma_G
\]

may encode the same order-four data twice.

In the worst case,

\[
r=-\frac{\Gamma_{G,2}}{\Gamma_G}
\]

identically, giving

\[
\ell_m(\Gamma)\equiv0.
\]

That would mean the \(Y12\)-normal covector is algebraically aligned with the actual source anisotropy, and the rank-one wall has no mass.

Thus the finite audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\partial_{q^{(4)}}r=0?
}
\]

or invariantly,

\[
\boxed{
r\text{ is measurable with respect to the low Gram/whitening algebra, not the }\Gamma_{G,2}\text{ variable.}
}
\]

---

# 5. How to audit \(\beta_9\)

The finite D4 derivation should be checked for the source of \(\beta_9\).

## Acceptable origins

\[
\beta_9
\]

may come from:

- expansion of Gram inverse factors;
- whitening normalization depending on \(g,h\);
- order-nine bookkeeping of the \(\Gamma_G\) channel;
- fixed scalar coefficients from the carrier basis;
- lower-order source variables already present in \(g,h\).

These keep \(r\) low-order.

## Dangerous origins

\[
\beta_9
\]

must not come from:

- substituting an actual order-nine source coefficient;
- using \(q^{(4)}\) or \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) to normalize the \(\Gamma_G\) channel;
- fitting the order-nine output to the actual \(s_9\);
- any coefficient extracted after applying the actual EF singular relation \(\mathcal K_9\).

If \(\beta_9\) has a dangerous origin, the moving wall is not a fixed algebraic direction and the anti-concentration theorem is compromised.

---

# 6. Relation to \(\beta_9\)-removal

Earlier we asked whether \(\beta_9\) can be removed. This audit changes the point.

If \(\beta_9\neq0\) but low-order, it is fine. The wall is moving but legitimate.

If \(\beta_9=0\), the wall is simpler.

If \(\beta_9\neq0\) and high-order/actual-source-dependent, the wall may be ill-posed.

So the priority is not making \(\beta_9\) vanish. The priority is proving:

\[
\boxed{
\beta_9\text{ is low-order and source-independent from }\Gamma_{G,2}.
}
\]

Then moving-wall finite type is a valid theorem.

---

# 7. Updated wall theorem with \(r\)-low-order hypothesis

The wall input should be stated as:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{MovingWallFiniteType}_{Y12}
}
\]

Assume:

1. \(\alpha_9\neq0\) and \(\alpha_9^{-1}\le Q^C\);
2. \(r=\beta_9/\alpha_9\) belongs to the low Gram algebra generated by \(g,h\) and fixed whitening coefficients;
3. \(r\) has polynomial \(C^J\) bounds;
4. \(q_{\can}\) is not finite-type trapped near

\[
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+r)q''
-
36rq_0
+
12rq_0^3;
\]

5. joint sublevel of \((\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})\) controls the \(|\Gamma_G|\)-small region.

Then

\[
\int
\min(Q^A|\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}|^2,1)
\,d\nu
\ge
Q^{-B}\nu(\mathcal C).
\]

---

# 8. Why this is the right formulation

This theorem is robust to \(\beta_9\neq0\). It does not require a special quotient that kills the \(\Gamma_G\)-piece.

It only needs \(r\) to be fixed by the formal D4 carrier geometry independently of the actual fourth-order source response.

That matches the philosophy:

\[
\boxed{
\text{D4 supplies a formal forbidden normal direction;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{the actual source cannot remain tangent to that direction on large mass.}
}
\]

---

# 9. Concrete next finite audit

The next finite calculation is:

\[
\boxed{
\beta_9=\beta_9(g,h)\ ?}
\]

More precisely:

1. Express \(\beta_9\) in the D4 carrier basis.
2. Check whether it depends only on \(g,h\) and fixed coefficients.
3. Verify

\[
\partial_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\beta_9=0.
\]

4. If yes, record \(r=r(g,h)\), compute its branch-charge degree and derivative bounds.
5. If no, identify the dangerous dependence.

This should be done before proving wall anti-concentration.

---

# 10. Current verdict

The moving wall is viable if:

\[
\boxed{
r=\beta_9/\alpha_9
\text{ is low-order.}
}
\]

Then wall failure imposes a genuine fourth-order D4-wall ODE and anti-concentration becomes a finite-type source-rigidity theorem.

If \(r\) secretly contains \(q^{(4)}\) or actual order-nine EF information, the moving wall may be tautological and the \(Y12\) rank-one channel may fail despite algebraic nonzero rank.

# 1. Certification target

We need prove:

\[
\boxed{
r=\frac{\beta_9}{\alpha_9}
\in
\mathcal A_{\low}
}
\]

where

\[
\mathcal A_{\low}
=
\mathbf C(g,h,\sqrt g,\sqrt h,\text{fixed carrier constants})

or a similarly low carrier algebra generated by

\[
g=g_{\rm Gr},
\qquad
h=h_{\rm Gr}.

Here

g_{\rm Gr}=\frac{q_0}{\pi},

\[
h_{\rm Gr}
=
\frac1\pi
\left(
\frac13q_0^3+\frac16u
\right),
\qquad
u=q''_{\can}.

So acceptable dependence is on

\[
q_0,\quad q''_{\can},
\]

and fixed finite carrier geometry.

Unacceptable dependence is on

\[
q^{(4)},\quad \Gamma_{G,2},\quad s_9^{\actual},\quad
\mathcal K_9(y_p),
\quad
\text{or fitted actual-source order-nine data}.

inside the formal source-coordinate algebra.

---

# 2. Why \(\alpha_9\) is already certified

The known clean coefficient

\[
\alpha_9
=
\frac{1}
{64\pi^2\sqrt{gh}\,h^{3/2}(\sqrt g+\sqrt h)}
\]

is plainly in the low Gram algebra. It depends on \(g,h\), not on \(q^{(4)}\) or actual order-nine source data.

\[
\beta_9.
\]

If \(\beta_9\in\mathcal A_{\low}\), then

\[
r\in\mathcal A_{\low}.
\]

If \(\beta_9\notin\mathcal A_{\low}\), locate the offending term.

---

# 3. Where \(\beta_9\) should come from

The \(\Gamma_G\)-to-order-nine leakage coefficient \(\beta_9\) should arise from applying the finite carrier conversion to the lower anisotropy channel. Its acceptable sources are:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Gram inverse expansion}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{whitening normalization depending on }g,h
}
\]

\[
\boxed{

}

\[

}
\]

\[
\boxed{

}
\]

All of these are low-order.

The danger is if \(\beta_9\) is obtained by comparing the actual order-nine coefficient against the actual order-seven coefficient. That would mean \(\beta_9\) is a fitted connection coefficient, not a formal carrier coefficient.

\text{Was }\beta_9\text{ computed before or after inserting actual }s_9\text{ data?}
}

# 4. Formal source-coordinate test

Introduce formal independent variables:

\[
X:=\Gamma_G,
\qquad
Y:=\Gamma_{G,2}.
\]

The clean D4 output should have the form

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
B_7\\
B_9
\end{pmatrix}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7&0\\
\beta_9&\alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}
\begin{pmatrix}
X\\
Y
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

This is a linear map over the coefficient ring

\[
\mathcal A_{\low}.
\]

The certification is:

\[
\boxed{
B_9/H
\in
\mathcal A_{\low}X+\mathcal A_{\low}Y
}
\]

with

\[
\boxed{
\beta_9=[X](B_9/H),
\qquad
\alpha_9=[Y](B_9/H).
}
\]

In this formulation, \(\beta_9\) is low-order by construction.

\[

contains terms such as

\]

or coefficients depending on \(Y\), then the map is not linear over the low coefficient ring and the clean D4 matrix interpretation fails.

Thus the audit is algebraic:

\[

\text{expand }B_9/H\text{ in independent variables }X,Y
\text{ over }\mathcal A_{\low}.
}
\]

---

# 5. Consequence for the wall ODE

If \(r\in\mathcal A_{\low}\), then

\[
f
=
\Gamma_{G,2}+r\Gamma_G
\]

is

\[
f
=
(1-q_0^2+r)u-\frac16q^{(4)}
-6rq_0+2rq_0^3.
\]

So \(f=0\) is

\[
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+r)u
-36rq_0
+12rq_0^3.
\]

Because \(r=r(q_0,u)\), this is genuinely fourth-order:

\[
\boxed{
q^{(4)}
=
F(q_0,q''_{\can}).
}
\]

If \(r\) is low-order and smooth with polynomial derivatives, persistent wall failure forces \(q_{\can}\) into a finite-dimensional ODE solution family.

That is exactly the rigidity needed for wall anti-concentration.

---

# 6. If \(r\) depends on \(q^{(4)}\)

Suppose

\[
r=r_0(q_0,u)+r_1(q_0,u)q^{(4)}.
\]

Then

\[
f=\Gamma_{G,2}+r\Gamma_G
\]

contains

\[
-\frac16q^{(4)}+r_1(q_0,u)\Gamma_G q^{(4)}.
\]

If

\[
r_1\Gamma_G=\frac16,
\]

the \(q^{(4)}\) coefficient cancels and wall finite-type degenerates.

Worse, if

\[
r=-\Gamma_{G,2}/\Gamma_G,
\]

then

\[
f\equiv0.
\]

So the dangerous condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\frac{\partial r}{\partial q^{(4)}}\neq0.
}
\]

Even partial \(q^{(4)}\)-dependence weakens the ODE-rigidity argument.

Thus if \(\partial_{q^{(4)}}r\neq0\), the moving-wall theorem must be reformulated in terms of the remaining coefficient of \(q^{(4)}\):

\[
-\frac16+\Gamma_G\partial_{q^{(4)}}r.
\]

Need this coefficient bounded below:

\[
\boxed{
\left|
-\frac16+\Gamma_G\partial_{q^{(4)}}r
\right|
\ge Q^{-C}.
}
\]

But the clean route is to prove

\[
\partial_{q^{(4)}}r=0.
\]

---

# 7. Branch-charge consequence

If

\[
r=r(g,h)
\]

with

\[
g,h
\]

low-order branch-neutral or bounded-degree branch-charge variables, then

\[
d(r)=O(1).
\]

Then

\[
d_\Gamma
=
d(\Gamma_{G,2}+r\Gamma_G)
=
O(1).
\]

So

\[
d_0=d_N+2d_H+2d_\Gamma
\]

stays \(O(1)\). This keeps O2/O3 near-pure survival finite-dimensional.

If \(r\) depends on actual high-order source data, its branch-charge degree may grow or become undefined, and the bounded-charge O2/O3 reduction is compromised.

So \(r\)-low-order is needed for both:

\[
\boxed{
\text{wall anti-concentration}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\text{bounded-charge O2/O3}.
}
\]

This is an important cross-dependency.

---

To certify \(r\), perform:

## Step 1 — identify the coefficient ring

Define

\[
\mathcal A_{\low}
=
\mathbf C[g,h,\sqrt g,\sqrt h,(\sqrt g+\sqrt h)^{-1},g^{-1},h^{-1}]
\]

localized on Gram-good patches.

## Step 2 — express \(B_9/H\)

Write \(B_9/H\) as a formal expression in

\]

with

\]

Confirm the derivation of \(\beta_9\) did not use:

\[
s_9^{\actual},
\quad
q^{(4)},
\quad
\Gamma_{G,2},
\quad
\mathcal K_9(y_p).
\]

## Step 5 — compute \(r\) branch-degree and derivatives

Need

\[
|r|,\ |r'|,\ldots,|r^{(J)}|\le Q^C.
\]

## Step 6 — insert into wall theorem

Then define

\[
f=\Gamma_{G,2}+r\Gamma_G.
\]

Proceed with \(\mathrm{D4WallFiniteType}(r)\).

---

# 9. If the audit fails

If \(\beta_9\) is not low-order, there are three possibilities.

## Case A — benign high-order dependence

If dependence is high-order but independent of \(q^{(4)}\), still may be okay, though branch-charge degree may increase.

## Case B — direct \(q^{(4)}\) dependence

Then wall ODE changes and may become tautological. Need a new nondegeneracy condition on the \(q^{(4)}\)-coefficient.

## Case C — fitted actual \(s_9\) dependence

Then the wall is not a formal D4 normal direction. It is fitted to actual source data. This likely kills the rank-one D4 route.

---

The moving-wall route remains viable if the following finite certification holds:

\[
\boxed{
\beta_9\in\mathcal A_{\low}
\quad\text{and}\quad
\partial_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\beta_9=0.
}

# 2. Failure types

## Arithmetic absorption

\[
y_Y\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\]

or

\[
y_Y\in \mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}.
\]

Meaning: \(Y12\) is genuine actual-source curvature. Finite D4 through \(Y12\) dies conceptually.

## Nuisance overcollapse

\[
y_Y\in \mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

because the singular quotient forgets the origin-pair label.

This may be repairable by refining the quotient or using a labelled realization.

## Amplitude absorption

\(E_2^Y\) or another coherent model space contains arbitrary local amplitudes matching

\[
H\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma).
\]

Then local fiber separation exists, but the actual amplitude is absorbed.

## Wall ODE trap

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G
\]

has high-order flatness or persistent vanishing, forcing the D4 wall ODE.

Then D4 rank exists but has insufficient wall mass.

## O2/O3 graph failure

\[
1/m_{\can,+}
\]

is too polynomial-like on the active branch, or pure-plus determinant mass does not survive.

---

# 3. Best current path if \(Y12\) survives

The clean path is:

1. **Labelled transition**: prove \(y_Y\neq0\) in the labelled singular \(Y12\) sector.

2. **Fiber graph**: prove actual \(Y12\)-coherence is graph-like:

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\rm fixed}
\]

with no free vertical direction.

3. **No-local-free \(E_2^Y\)**: classify \(E_2^Y\) as absent/fixed/residual/finite-dimensional coherent, not locally free.

4. **Low-order \(\rho_\Gamma\)**:

\[
\rho_\Gamma
=
\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
\in\mathcal A_{\low},
\qquad
\partial_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\rho_\Gamma=0.
\]

5. **Moving-wall finite type** for

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G.
\]

6. **Compute \(d_0\)** from \(\ell_{\eff}\).

7. **O2/O3 fixed-\(\alpha\)** using CanonicalQuadraticMultiplier + Christoffel spread + NearPureMomentSeparation for \(R_{\fold}\).

8. **AggL2** via MultiscaleFoldRouting and finite AFE anti-lock.

9. **Atomic contradiction**.

---

# 4. Fallback paths if \(Y12\) fails

## Failure by arithmetic absorption

If \(Y12\) is legitimate actual-source curvature, move to:

\[
K_{11},
\]

another primitive carrier, or a higher-order D4 package.

## Failure by nuisance overcollapse

Refine singular quotient or use labelled transition realization. This is potentially repairable.

## Failure by \(E_2\) local freedom

Try to quotient around the finite-dimensional \(E_2^Y\) amplitude space if it is not fully local. If truly local-free, current \(Y12\) route dies.

## Failure by labelled transition mismatch

Use the full Schur test in the raw realized singular space instead of relying on signature labels.

## Failure by wall finite-type

If \(\ell_{\eff}\) is nonzero but wall mass fails, investigate the D4 wall ODE branch. It may either be a contradiction by another route or a no-go for finite D4.

---

}

## Agent 6 continued attack — graph-like \(Y12\) coherence versus amplitude absorption

I will keep the current nomenclature:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{lower }Y12\text{ graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The previous dependency graph says the highest-leverage fork is whether \(Y12\) coherence is **graph-like but vertically nonfree**. I now push that into a precise two-level theorem. 

---

# 1. Fiber-level theorem is not enough

In the primitive \(Y12\) fiber,

\[
(s_7,s_9^Y),
\]

actual-source coherence may impose

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\rm fixed}.
\]

D4 gives

\[
s_7^{D4}=Hc_7\Gamma_G,
\]

\[
s_9^{Y,D4}=H(\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

Subtracting the graph gives the fiber residual

\[
H\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)
=
H\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right).
\]

So fiber-level nonverticality gives a local normal direction.

But this alone does not close the contradiction. If actual-source completion \(E_2^Y\) or another coherent mechanism supplies a finite-dimensional amplitude space \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\), the normal fiber direction could still be absorbed as a function of \(m\):

\[
H\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)
\in
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Therefore the downstream theorem must be two-level:

\[
\boxed{
\text{fiber normality}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\text{amplitude-function transversality}.
}
\]

---

# 2. Fiber normality theorem

Define the local \(Y12\) fiber map

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}:H_Y\to \mathbb C^2,
\qquad
v\mapsto(s_7(v),s_9^Y(v)).
\]

Let the actual-source coherent fiber image be

\[
\mathcal F_Y^{\arith}(p)
\subset \mathbb C^2.
\]

The required theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal F_Y^{\arith}(p)
\text{ is graph-like with polynomial slope }\rho_Y(p),
}
\]

meaning

\[
\mathcal F_Y^{\arith}(p)
\subset
\{(s_7,s_9^Y):s_9^Y=\rho_Y(p)s_7+\eta_{\rm fixed}(p)\}
\]

up to residual-controlled error.

Equivalently, the vertical direction

\[
v_Y=(0,1)
\]

has polynomial distance from the tangent:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
v_Y,
T\mathcal F_Y^{\arith}(p)
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

This is the true local finite \(Y12\)-separation. It allows a lower \(7\to9\) connection; it only forbids free vertical motion.

---

# 3. Effective wall after graph subtraction

Once \(\rho_Y\) is fixed, the effective raw wall is

\[
\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)
=
(\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}.
\]

Normalize:

\[
\rho_\Gamma
=
\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
\]

Then

\[
\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)
=
\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

The key low-order certification must now be applied to \(\rho_\Gamma\), not to \(\beta_9/\alpha_9\):

\[
\boxed{
\rho_\Gamma\in\mathcal A_{\low},
\qquad
\partial_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\rho_\Gamma=0.
}
\]

This requires all three coefficients

\[
\beta_9,\quad c_7,\quad \rho_Y
\]

to be low-order, in addition to \(\alpha_9\).

---

# 4. Amplitude absorption theorem

Let

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

be the finite-dimensional coherent \(Y12\)-amplitude space coming from fixed/coherent \(E_2^Y\), admissible parameter families, or other actual-source coherent contributions.

Then the true wall lower is not just

\[
|H\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)|^2.
\]

It is the distance

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
\left(
H\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma),
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\right)^2.
}
\]

The needed theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
\left(
H\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma),
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\right)^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|H\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2.
}
\]

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0,
\]

this reduces to moving-wall anti-concentration.

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is finite-dimensional but nonzero, this is a finite Schur complement in function space.

Fatal case:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

contains arbitrary local amplitudes at extraction scale. Then the channel is locally free and dies.

---

# 5. Finite-dimensional coherent \(E_2^Y\) is not fatal

Suppose

\[
E_2^Y
\]

has a finite coherent expansion

\[
E_2^Y(p)
=
\sum_{j=1}^{M} a_j \phi_j(p),
\qquad
M\le Q^C,
\]

with \(\phi_j\) fixed structural amplitude functions and coefficients not locally arbitrary at each patch.

Then

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{E2}
=
\operatorname{span}\{\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_M\}
\]

is not fatal. It only changes the wall theorem to a distance-to-space estimate:

\[
H\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)
\notin
\mathcal C_Y^{E2}
\]

with polynomial angle.

So the correct \(E_2\) condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoLocalFreeE2Y12}
}
\]

not

\[
E_2^Y=0.
\]

This is a useful relaxation.

---

# 6. Wall finite type modulo coherent amplitudes

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\neq0\), the moving-wall finite-type theorem should be stated modulo \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\):

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
\left(
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G),
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\right)^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2.
}
\]

A sufficient condition is that no nontrivial coherent amplitude

\[
\phi\in \mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

makes

\[
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)-\phi
\]

flat to high order on polynomial mass.

This is again a finite-type theorem, but now for a finite family:

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G-\phi/(H\alpha_9).
\]

If \(M=O(1)\) or \(Q^C\), cover \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) by polynomial nets after normalization and apply finite-type estimates uniformly.

---

# 7. Wall ODE modulo coherent amplitudes

Without amplitude space, wall failure gives

\[
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+\rho_\Gamma)q''
-36\rho_\Gamma q_0
+12\rho_\Gamma q_0^3.
\]

With amplitude \(\phi\), the equation becomes

\[
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)=\phi.
\]

Equivalently,

\[
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+\rho_\Gamma)q''
-36\rho_\Gamma q_0
+12\rho_\Gamma q_0^3
-
6\frac{\phi}{H\alpha_9}.
\]

Thus amplitude absorption corresponds to trapping near a **forced D4 wall ODE** with forcing term

\[
-6\frac{\phi}{H\alpha_9}.
\]

So the wall theorem modulo coherent amplitudes is:

\[
\boxed{
q_{\can}
\text{ is not trapped near any forced D4-wall ODE with forcing }\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
}
\]

This is the exact analytic obstruction.

---

# 8. Dependency impact

The dependency graph should be refined:

\[
\mathrm{Y12FiberGraph}
\Rightarrow
\rho_Y
\Rightarrow
\rho_\Gamma
\Rightarrow
\ell_{\raw}.
\]

Then:

\[

\Rightarrow
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\Rightarrow
\ell_{\eff}
=
\ell_{\raw}\ \mathrm{mod}\ \mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Then:

\[

\Rightarrow
d_{\Gamma,\eff}
\Rightarrow
d_0.
\]

So \(d_0\) cannot be computed from \(\beta_9,\alpha_9\) alone. It depends on:

1. \(\rho_Y\);
2. coherent \(E_2^Y\)-amplitude subtraction;
3. final effective wall degree.

finite-dimensional, or locally free.

=

has polynomial finite type on most mass.

modulo the coherent \(Y12\)-amplitude space \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\). This means the wall layer is itself another finite Schur problem, not merely a scalar sublevel problem. This is the main refinement from the last round. 

---

# 1. Effective wall residual

Define

\[
F_{\wall}(m)
:=
H(m)\left((\beta_9(m)-\rho_Y(m)c_7(m))\Gamma_G(m)
+\alpha_9(m)\Gamma_{G,2}(m)\right).
\]

Equivalently,

\[
F_{\wall}
=
H\alpha_9
\left(
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G
\right).
\]

If there is no coherent \(E_2^Y\)-amplitude space, then the wall is simply \(F_{\wall}\).

If there is a finite coherent amplitude space

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
=
\operatorname{span}\{\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_M\},
\qquad M\le Q^C,
\]

then the effective wall is

\[
\boxed{
F_{\wall}^{\eff}
:=
(I-P_{\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}})F_{\wall}.
}
\]

The required wall lower is

\[
\boxed{
\|F_{\wall}^{\eff}\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2
\ge Q^{-A}\|F_{\wall}\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2.
}
\]

This is the correct replacement for naive pointwise anti-concentration if coherent \(E_2^Y\) amplitudes exist.

---

# 2. Wall Schur complement

Let

\[
\Phi=(\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_M)
\]

be a column matrix for \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\). Then

\[
\|F_{\wall}^{\eff}\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2
=
\|F_{\wall}\|^2
-
F_{\wall}^*\Phi(\Phi^*\Phi)^\dagger\Phi^*F_{\wall}.
\]

If \(\Phi^*\Phi\) is nonsingular, this is the Schur complement

\[
\boxed{
\delta_{\wall}^2
=
\frac{\det G_{\Phi,F_{\wall}}}{\det G_\Phi}.
}
\]

Thus the wall layer has the same shape as the \(Y12\)-fiber separation and the O2/O3 near-pure separation:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}(F_{\wall},\mathcal C_Y^{\arith})
\ge Q^{-A}\|F_{\wall}\|.
}
\]

This is useful: the downstream obstruction is now a **two-stage Schur separation**.

First:

\[
y_Y\notin\mathcal A
\]

in the finite \(Y12\) carrier fiber.

Second:

\[
F_{\wall}\notin\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

as a function on the routed set.

Both must hold.

---

# 3. When \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is harmless

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is finite-dimensional and low-complexity, it should be harmless unless it is specifically tuned to the wall.

A sufficient theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\forall \phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith},\ \|\phi\|=1,\quad
F_{\wall}-\phi
\text{ has polynomial finite type on most mass.}
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
F_{\wall}
\]

does not lie near the finite-dimensional coherent amplitude model.

This is a forced-wall finite-type theorem.

---

# 4. Forced D4 wall ODE

If

\[
F_{\wall}\approx \phi
\]

for some \(\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\), then

\[
H\alpha_9
(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)
\approx
\phi.
\]

Assuming \(H\alpha_9\) is dyadically frozen and nonzero, this becomes

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G
\approx
\frac{\phi}{H\alpha_9}.
\]

Using

\[
\Gamma_G=u-6q_0+2q_0^3,
\]

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=(1-q_0^2)u-\frac16q^{(4)},
\]

we get the forced D4 wall ODE:

\[
\boxed{
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+\rho_\Gamma)q''
-36\rho_\Gamma q_0
+12\rho_\Gamma q_0^3
-
6\frac{\phi}{H\alpha_9}.
}
\]

So amplitude absorption is equivalent to trapping near a finite-dimensional family of forced fourth-order ODEs.

This is a precise analytic obstruction.

---

# 5. Effective finite-type theorem

The wall theorem should now be stated as:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4WallFiniteTypeModuloCoherence}(\rho_\Gamma,\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}).
}
\]

For every normalized

\[
\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith},
\]

the function

\[
F_\phi
:=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)-\phi
\]

has polynomial finite type on most of the post-Gate/O2O3 smooth measure \(\nu\).

Then

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
\left(
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G),
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\right)^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2.
\]

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0\), this reduces to the scalar moving-wall theorem.

---

# 6. Low-order requirement now includes \(\rho_Y\)

The low-order audit must certify

\[
\rho_\Gamma
=
\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
\in\mathcal A_{\low},
\]

not merely

\[
\beta_9/\alpha_9\in\mathcal A_{\low}.
\]

Thus we need:

\[

\beta_9,\alpha_9,c_7,\rho_Y\in\mathcal A_{\low}.
}
\]

Known:

\[
c_7,\alpha_9\in\mathcal A_{\low}
\]

from the Gram formulas.

Still needed:

\[
\boxed{
\beta_9\in\mathcal A_{\low},
\qquad
\rho_Y\in\mathcal A_{\low}.
}
\]

And critically:

\[
\boxed{
\partial_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\rho_\Gamma=0.
}
\]

If \(\rho_Y\) is fitted from actual \(s_9^Y\)-data, then \(\rho_\Gamma\) may become tautological. If \(\rho_Y\) is a formal graph coefficient from lower singular coherence, then the wall is honest.

Acceptable:

\[
\rho_Y
\]

comes from:

- Gram/whitening coefficients;

comes from:

- using actual order-nine EF data;
- using \(E_2^Y\)-amplitude at the local patch;
- depending on \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) or \(q^{(4)}\).

The certification target is:

\[
\boxed{
\rho_Y\in\mathcal A_{\low},
\qquad
\partial_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\rho_Y=0.
}
\]

If this holds, then \(\rho_\Gamma\) is low-order once \(\beta_9\) is certified.

---

# 8. Amplitude-space dimension and locality

The coherent \(E_2^Y\)-space is fatal only if locally free. Make this precise.

Let \(p\) index local atoms. If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

has basis functions \(\phi_j(p)\), then it is harmless when:

1. \(M=\dim\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\le Q^C\);
2. the \(\phi_j\) have polynomial complexity;
3. \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) does not contain functions localized to individual extraction atoms;
4. \(F_{\wall}\) has finite type modulo \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\).

It is fatal when for every local atom \(p\) the space contains an independent bump

\[
\phi_p
\]

supported near \(p\). Then it can absorb any local D4 amplitude and no atomic contradiction remains.

Thus the exact theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\text{ has no extraction-scale local basis.}
}
\]

This is the functional version of “no local free \(E_2^Y\).”

---

# 9. Revised atomic contradiction

The upper is now

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
\left(
\Pi_YJ_{\sing}(q''_{\can}),
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\right)^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu(\mathcal C).
}
\]

The lower from D4 is

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
\left(
F_{\wall},
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\right)^2
\ge Q^{-A}\mu(\mathcal G).
}
\]

So the contradiction compares distance-to-model upper and lower. It is not simply a raw squared norm unless \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0\).

This is the correct final form.

---

# 10. Updated dependency order

The downstream order is now:

1. **Labelled transition/quotient faithfulness**: \(y_Y\neq0\).

2. **Fiber graph theorem**: actual-source \(Y12\) coherence is graph-like, yielding \(\rho_Y\), not vertically free.

3. **Coherent amplitude classification**: determine \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\), especially \(E_2^Y\).

4. **Low-order certification**:

\[
\rho_\Gamma=(\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)/\alpha_9\in\mathcal A_{\low}.

5. **Wall finite type modulo \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\)**.

6. **Compute \(d_0\)** from \(\ell_{\eff}\), not \(\ell_{\raw}\).

Only after these should the O2/O3 near-pure Schur block be finalized.

---

\boxed{
\mathrm{Y12GraphNormalD4}.

(0,1)\notin T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith};

## Agent 6 continued attack — merge fiber graph and amplitude absorption into one Schur problem

The previous attack separated two layers:

\[
\text{fiber graph normality}
\quad+\quad
\text{amplitude-function transversality}.
\]

That is correct, but it can be made cleaner. The entire downstream \(Y12\) obstruction can be represented as **one block Schur complement** in a mixed fiber–amplitude Hilbert space. This removes ambiguity about whether \(E_2^Y\), lower connection, or graph coherence is being handled “locally” or “globally.” 

---

# 1. Build the mixed \(Y12\) observation vector

On each transition–singular atom \(p\), the primitive \(Y12\) fiber has coordinates

\[
(s_7(p),s_9^Y(p)).
\]

D4 contributes

\[
s_7^{D4}(p)=H(p)c_7(p)\Gamma_G(p),
\]

\[
s_9^{Y,D4}(p)
=
H(p)\bigl(\beta_9(p)\Gamma_G(p)+\alpha_9(p)\Gamma_{G,2}(p)\bigr).
\]

The coherent graph relation is

\[
s_9^Y(p)=\rho_Y(p)s_7(p)+\eta_{\rm fixed}(p).
\]

Subtracting this graph gives the local vertical residual

\[
V_Y^{D4}(p)
=
H(p)\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right)(p).
\]

Equivalently,

\[
V_Y^{D4}(p)
=
H(p)\alpha_9(p)
\left(\Gamma_{G,2}(p)+\rho_\Gamma(p)\Gamma_G(p)\right).
\]

This \(V_Y^{D4}\) is the **actual downstream amplitude** that must avoid the coherent \(Y12\)-amplitude model.

---

# 2. Define the coherent amplitude model

Let

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
=
\operatorname{span}\{\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_M\}
\]

be the coherent \(Y12\)-amplitude space coming from:

\[
E_2^Y\text{ fixed/coherent components},
\]

\[
\text{allowed finite-dimensional source-family amplitudes},
\]

\[
\text{fixed basepoint shifts after graph subtraction}.
\]

The fatal case is not \(M>0\). The fatal case is that \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is locally free at extraction scale.

So require:

\[
M\le Q^C,
\]

and no basis of \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) consists of independent atom-local bumps.

---

# 3. One Schur complement for the effective wall

Define the amplitude vector

\[
F_Y(p):=V_Y^{D4}(p).
\]

Let \(\Phi\) be the \(p\times M\) matrix with columns \(\phi_j\). Then the effective wall energy is

\[
\boxed{
\delta_Y^2
=
\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2
-
F_Y^*\Phi(\Phi^*\Phi)^\dagger\Phi^*F_Y.
}
\]

If \(\Phi^*\Phi\) is nonsingular,

\[
\boxed{
\delta_Y^2
=
\frac{\det G_{\Phi,F_Y}}{\det G_\Phi}.
}
\]

This is the precise amplitude-level analogue of the local fiber Schur residual.

The downstream wall theorem becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\delta_Y^2
\ge
Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2.
}
\]

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0\), this is just

\[
\|F_Y\|^2\ge Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|^2,
\]

and the remaining problem is ordinary sublevel/finite-type mass for \(F_Y\).

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\neq0\), this is the needed “no amplitude absorption” theorem.

---

# 4. Forced wall ODE with coherent amplitudes

For any coherent amplitude

\[
\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith},
\]

absorption means

\[
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)\approx \phi.
\]

Using

\[
\Gamma_G=q''_{\can}-6q_0+2q_0^3,
\]

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=(1-q_0^2)q''_{\can}-\frac16q^{(4)},
\]

we get the forced wall equation

\[
\boxed{
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+\rho_\Gamma)q''
-36\rho_\Gamma q_0
+12\rho_\Gamma q_0^3
-
6\frac{\phi}{H\alpha_9}.
}
\]

Thus the wall theorem modulo \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is exactly:

\[
\boxed{
q_{\can}
\text{ is not trapped near any forced D4-wall ODE with forcing }
\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
}
\]

This is the correct analytic form.

---

# 5. Local-free criterion for \(E_2^Y\)

The coherent amplitude model is acceptable if it is low-complexity. It is fatal if it contains atom-local basis functions.

A clean criterion:

For atoms \(p\), let \(P_{\le L}\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) denote the restriction to any cluster of \(L\) adjacent atoms. If

\[
\dim(P_{\le L}\mathcal C_Y^{\arith})\asymp L
\]

for \(L\) as small as the extraction scale, then \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is locally free.

If instead

\[
\dim(P_{\le L}\mathcal C_Y^{\arith})\le Q^C
\]

globally and grows slowly with \(L\), then it is coherent, not fatal.

So a useful theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoLocalFreeE2Y12}:
\quad
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\text{ has no extraction-scale local basis.}
}
\]

This is more precise than “\(E_2^Y=0\).”

---

# 6. Effective wall finite type modulo \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\)

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is finite-dimensional, prove the wall lower by finite-type uniformly over \(\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\).

Normalize \(\phi\) and consider

\[
F_\phi
=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)-\phi.
\]

Need:

\[
\boxed{
F_\phi
\text{ has polynomial finite type on most of }\nu
\text{ uniformly for all normalized }\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
}
\]

Then

\[
\operatorname{dist}(F_Y,\mathcal C_Y^{\arith})
\ge Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|.
\]

If \(M=\dim\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\le Q^C\), one can reduce uniformity to a polynomial net, provided the finite-type constants vary polynomially.

---

# 7. \(\rho_\Gamma\) low-order remains mandatory

The forced ODE is meaningful only if

\[
\rho_\Gamma
=
\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
\]

is low-order:

\[
\rho_\Gamma\in \mathcal A_{\low},
\qquad
\partial_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\rho_\Gamma=0.
\]

This means:

\[
\beta_9,\rho_Y,c_7,\alpha_9
\]

must all be formal carrier/Gram coefficients, not fitted actual order-nine source data.

Known:

\[
c_7,\alpha_9\in\mathcal A_{\low}.
\]

Still to certify:

\[
\beta_9\in\mathcal A_{\low},
\qquad
\rho_Y\in\mathcal A_{\low}.
\]

If \(\rho_Y\) is fitted from actual \(s_9^Y/s_7\), then \(\rho_\Gamma\) may become tautological. If \(\rho_Y\) is a formal lower-connection graph coefficient, the wall remains honest.

---

# 8. Updated downstream theorem

The rank-one \(Y12\) downstream theorem should be stated as:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{Y12GraphNormalD4}
}
\]

with hypotheses:

1. **Labelled survival**:

\[
y_Y=T_{\tr\to\sing}[\mathsf C_{Y12}]\neq0
\]

in the primitive \(Y12\)-signature quotient.

2. **Fiber graph nonverticality**:

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\rm fixed}
\]

with no free vertical direction.

3. **No local free \(E_2^Y\)**:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

is absent, fixed, residual-controlled, or finite-dimensional coherent, but not extraction-local.

4. **Low-order wall ratio**:

\[
\rho_\Gamma\in\mathcal A_{\low},
\qquad
\partial_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\rho_\Gamma=0.
\]

5. **Effective wall Schur lower**:

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
\left(
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G),
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\right)^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)\|^2.
\]

Then D4 has a valid atomic forbidden channel.

---

# 9. How this affects \(d_0\)

Only after the effective wall residual is fixed can we compute

\[
d_{\Gamma,\eff}.
\]

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0,
\]

then

\[
d_{\Gamma,\eff}
=
d\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right).
\]

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\neq0,
\]

then \(d_{\Gamma,\eff}\) must account for the projection or Schur residual against \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\). If the projection introduces only fixed finite-dimensional low-complexity amplitudes, \(d_{\Gamma,\eff}\) remains \(O(1)\). If it introduces rough local functions, the O2/O3 bounded-charge route breaks.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
d_0=d_N+2d_H+2d_{\Gamma,\eff}
}
\]

is downstream of the \(Y12\)-amplitude model, not just of \(Y12\)-fiber survival.

---

# 10. Main new no-go

A new failure mode appears:

Even if \(Y12\) is fiber-normal,

\[
(0,1)\notin T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith},
\]

the route can still fail if

\[
H\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)
\in
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

as an amplitude function.

This is amplitude absorption.

So the full obstruction is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{fiber normality}
+
\text{amplitude non-absorption}.
}
\]

Both are necessary.

---

# 11. Next precise attack

The next mathematical task is to classify

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Specifically:

1. Is \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0\)?
2. If not, what is its dimension?
3. Is it low-complexity and nonlocal?
4. Does it contain \(H\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)\)?
5. Does projecting away \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) preserve bounded branch-charge degree?

This is now as important as the original \(Y12\)-signature survival.

## Agent 6 continued attack — classifying \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\)

The previous step exposed the real downstream condition:

\[
\text{fiber normality}
+
\text{amplitude non-absorption}.
\]

Fiber normality says the local \(Y12\)-fiber is graph-like, not vertically free. Amplitude non-absorption says the actual D4 wall amplitude

\[
F_Y(m)
=
H(m)\alpha_9(m)\bigl(\Gamma_{G,2}(m)+\rho_\Gamma(m)\Gamma_G(m)\bigr)
\]

does not lie in the coherent \(Y12\)-amplitude space

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

So the next object to classify is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
}
\]

This is now as important as the local \(Y12\)-signature survival. The latest prompt explicitly makes the route depend on distinguishing graph coherence, local freedom, and effective wall residuals. 

---

# 1. What \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is allowed to contain

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

should contain only coherent actual-source \(Y12\)-amplitude functions. It may include:

\[
\boxed{
\text{fixed }E_2^Y\text{ basepoint shifts}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{finite-dimensional coherent completion amplitudes}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{global parameter amplitudes from admissible actual-source families}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{chart/normalization amplitudes that are globally constrained}
}
\]

It must not include:

\[
\boxed{
\text{one freely chosen }Y12\text{ amplitude per extraction atom}
}
\]

or anything equivalent to arbitrary local interpolation at the patch scale.

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) contains arbitrary local bump functions at the same scale as the D4 atoms, then

\[
F_Y\in \mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

automatically after fitting, and the forbidden channel disappears.

So the first theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoLocalFreeE2Y12}:
\quad
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\text{ has no extraction-scale local basis.}
}
\]

---

# 2. Four cases for \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\)

## Case A: \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0\)

This is ideal.

Then the effective wall is simply

\[
F_Y=H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

The downstream wall theorem is ordinary moving-wall finite type.

## Case B: fixed basepoint only

If

\[
E_2^Y=e_Y^{\fixed}(p)
\]

is fixed by the actual source construction and does not vary freely, then it shifts the coherent graph basepoint

\[
\eta_{\fixed}.
\]

It does **not** enter the tangent or amplitude space. This is also safe.

The wall remains

\[
F_Y.
\]

## Case C: finite-dimensional coherent space

Suppose

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
=
\operatorname{span}\{\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_M\},
\qquad
M\le Q^C,
\]

where the \(\phi_j\) are structural global/semiglobal amplitude functions.

Then the wall theorem becomes the Schur complement

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
\left(
F_Y,\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\right)^2
\ge
Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2.
\]

This is viable if the \(\phi_j\) are low-complexity and not tuned to \(F_Y\).

## Case D: locally free amplitude space

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

contains atom-local basis functions

\[
\phi_p\approx \mathbf 1_p
\]

or any comparable family with independently adjustable amplitudes at extraction scale, then

\[
F_Y
\]

can be absorbed locally.

This is fatal:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\text{ locally free}
\Longrightarrow
\text{current }Y12\text{ channel fails.}
}
\]

---

# 3. A dimension/locality test

Let \(P_\Omega\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) denote restriction to a cluster \(\Omega\) of \(L\) adjacent atoms.

A coherent finite-dimensional amplitude space should satisfy something like

\[
\dim P_\Omega\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\le Q^C
\]

uniformly, with much slower growth than \(L\) at extraction scales.

A locally free space has

\[
\dim P_\Omega\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\asymp L
\]

for clusters as small as the extraction scale.

Thus a clean criterion is:

\[
\boxed{
\sup_{\Omega}
\frac{\dim P_\Omega\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}}{\#\Omega}
\le Q^{-A}
\quad
\text{at extraction scales.}
}
\]

Or, more practically:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\text{ admits no basis with polynomially localized atom support.}
}
\]

This is the functional version of “not vertically free.”

---

# 4. If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is finite-dimensional, wall absorption is a forced ODE family

For

\[
\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith},
\]

absorption means

\[
F_Y\approx \phi.
\]

That is,

\[
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)\approx \phi.
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G\approx \frac{\phi}{H\alpha_9}.
\]

Using

\[
\Gamma_G=q''_{\can}-6q_0+2q_0^3,
\]

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=(1-q_0^2)q''_{\can}-\frac16q^{(4)},
\]

we obtain the forced wall ODE

\[
\boxed{
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+\rho_\Gamma)q''
-36\rho_\Gamma q_0
+12\rho_\Gamma q_0^3
-
6\frac{\phi}{H\alpha_9}.
}
\]

Thus amplitude absorption is equivalent to trapping near a finite-dimensional family of forced D4-wall ODEs.

So the needed theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
q_{\can}
\text{ is not trapped near any forced D4-wall ODE with }
\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
}
\]

---

# 5. Schur formulation of amplitude non-absorption

Let

\[
F_Y(p)=H(p)\alpha_9(p)
\left(\Gamma_{G,2}(p)+\rho_\Gamma(p)\Gamma_G(p)\right).
\]

Let

\[
\Phi=(\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_M)
\]

span

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Then

\[
\operatorname{dist}(F_Y,\mathcal C_Y^{\arith})^2
=
\|F_Y\|^2-
F_Y^*\Phi(\Phi^*\Phi)^\dagger\Phi^*F_Y.
\]

If \(\Phi^*\Phi\) is nonsingular,

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}(F_Y,\mathcal C_Y^{\arith})^2
=
\frac{\det G_{\Phi,F_Y}}{\det G_\Phi}.
}
\]

So the amplitude layer is exactly a finite Schur complement, parallel to:

\[
y_Y\notin\mathcal A
\]

and

\[
D_{-n}\notin \operatorname{span}\{D_{-n+1},\ldots,D_{-n+d_0}\}.
\]

The entire proof is now a chain of finite Schur separations plus observability estimates.

---

# 6. How to prove the amplitude Schur lower

A sufficient route:

1. Normalize \(\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) with \(\|\phi\|=1\).

2. Prove uniform finite type for

\[
F_Y-\phi.
\]

3. Cover the unit sphere of \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) by a polynomial net.

4. Apply sublevel/finite-type estimates uniformly.

This works if

\[
M=\dim \mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\le O(\log Q)
\]

or at least \(M\le Q^C\) with sufficiently strong net control.

If \(M\) is too large, one needs structure: for example, \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is itself a low-degree polynomial/exponential family and admits a Remez-type inequality.

Thus the theorem should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{ForcedWallFiniteType}
(\rho_\Gamma,\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}).
}
\]

---

# 7. How \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) affects \(d_0\)

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0,
\]

then

\[
d_{\Gamma,\eff}
=
d\left(
(\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\right).
\]

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\neq0,
\]

then the wall used in the smooth pre-extraction O2/O3 weight is not just raw \(F_Y\). The correct object is the residual

\[
(I-P_{\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}})F_Y.
\]

This projection may introduce nonlocal amplitudes. If those amplitudes are not bounded-degree in branch charge, the O2/O3 bounded-charge reduction may break.

Therefore require:

\[
\boxed{
P_{\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}}
\text{ has bounded branch-charge complexity,}
}
\]

or perform O2/O3 before projecting away \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) and then handle amplitude absorption later. The cleaner route is:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0
\]

or finite-dimensional branch-neutral/low-charge.

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) has high branch-charge complexity, \(d_0\) may no longer be \(O(1)\).

This is a new dependency.

---

# 8. Recommended definition of \(\ell_{\eff}\)

Define the effective wall in two stages.

## Stage 1 — local fiber residual

\[
\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma)
=
(\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}.
\]

## Stage 2 — amplitude residual

\[
F_Y=H\ell_{\raw}(\Gamma).
\]

Then

\[
\boxed{
F_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_{\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}})F_Y.
}
\]

The wall factor should be

\[
\boxed{
E_{\wall}^{Y12}
=
\frac{|F_Y^{\eff}|^2}{\mathcal N_{\D4}}
}
\]

in the atomized/square-sum sense.

If the projection is nonlocal, define the wall at aggregate level before pointwise extraction:

\[
\sum_p |F_Y^{\eff}(p)|^2.
\]

Only if \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0\) or the projection is local enough can one use a pointwise wall factor.

This is important: amplitude projection may destroy pointwise locality.

---

# 9. Locality of the amplitude projection

For the atomic contradiction, we prefer local atoms. If

\[
P_{\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}}
\]

is global, then

\[
F_Y^{\eff}(p)
\]

depends on all atoms.

This reintroduces complement-cancellation-like issues.

Therefore, for the clean Type-I atomic contradiction, require one of:

## Option A: \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0\)

Best.

## Option B: \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) decomposes locally

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
=
\bigoplus_{\omega}
\mathcal C_{Y,\omega}^{\arith}
\]

over blocks \(\omega\) of polynomial size, and projection is local to each block.

## Option C: perform contradiction at block level

Use block atoms \(\omega\) instead of point atoms \(p\):

\[
\sum_\omega
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
F_Y|_\omega,
\mathcal C_{Y,\omega}^{\arith}
\right)^2.
\]

This remains Type-I if blocks have bounded overlap.

If none holds, amplitude projection makes the downstream less local and more fragile.

---

# 10. Updated failure modes

The \(Y12\) route can now fail by:

1. **fiber free verticality**:

\[
(0,1)\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
\]

2. **amplitude local freedom**:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

contains extraction-scale local bumps.

3. **amplitude high-complexity**:

\[
P_{\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}}
\]

has high branch charge or nonlocal dependence that breaks O2/O3 and atomicity.

4. **forced wall ODE trapping**:

\[
F_Y\approx \phi
\]

for some coherent amplitude \(\phi\).

5. **low-order failure**:

\[
\rho_\Gamma
\]

depends on \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) or actual \(s_9\), making wall tautological.

---

# 11. Current best simplification

If agents can prove:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0
\quad\text{or fixed basepoint only,}
}
\]

then the downstream becomes much simpler:

\[
F_Y^{\eff}=F_Y,
\]

\[
d_{\Gamma,\eff}=d(\ell_{\raw}),
\]

\[
\text{atomic contradiction is pointwise/local,}
\]

and the wall theorem is just finite type for

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G.
\]

So after \(Y12\)-fiber survival, the next highest-value task is to prove:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\text{ is zero/fixed, not finite-dimensional nonlocal.}
}
\]

That is stronger than NoLocalFreeE2Y12 and would greatly simplify the proof.

---

# 12. Final status

The downstream theorem is now:

\[
\boxed{
\text{fiber graph normality}
+
\text{amplitude non-absorption}
+
\text{low-order effective wall}
+
\text{wall finite type}.
}
\]

The true effective wall is

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_{\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}})
\left[
H\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right)
\right].
\]

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0\), this collapses to the moving-wall finite-type theorem. If not, the proof must handle a block/Schur wall residual against \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\).

## Agent 6 — updated dependency graph and branch-decision memo

The architecture is now:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{Agg}L^2(q''_{\can})
+

# 1. Dependency graph

## A. \(\mathrm{LiveY12DualPair}\)

Construct bounded covectors

\[
\ell_7,\qquad \ell_9^Y
\]

such that, in the realized singular quotient,

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)([\mathsf C_7])=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)([\mathsf C_{Y12}])=(0,1).
\]

Equivalent finite condition: after projecting away nuisance/source-linear directions, the live Gram matrix

\[
G_{\live}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\langle e_7,e_7\rangle&\langle e_7,e_Y\rangle\\
\langle e_Y,e_7\rangle&\langle e_Y,e_Y\rangle
\end{pmatrix}
\]

has

\[
\lambda_{\min}(G_{\live})\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

**Status:** critical blocker.

If this fails, the \(Y12\) channel cannot even be coordinatized reliably.

---

## B. \(\mathrm{LabelledTransitionY12}\)

Need labelled transition

\[
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
\]

and realization

\[
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\]

such that

\[
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}J_{\tr}(q''_{\can})
=
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}J_{\tr}(q''_{\can})
+
R_{\lab},
\]

with

\[
\Pi_YR_{\lab}
\]

absent, fixed, residual-controlled, or finite-dimensional coherent, but not locally free.

This protects the origin-pair label

\[
(o_N,o_W)=(D^2N_0,D\mathcal W_{\same}).
\]

**Status:** critical blocker.

If labelled transition fails only because the quotient is too coarse, refine the quotient. If it fails because the labelled object does not realize the actual singular row, the labelled route is invalid.

---

## C. \(\mathrm{Y12FiberGraphSchur}\)

Project actual-source allowed directions to the two-coordinate fiber

\[
(s_7,s_9^Y).
\]

Let the allowed fiber matrix be

\[
B_Y.
\]

Classify:

\[
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=0:
\quad
\rho_Y=0.
\]

\[
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=1,\ \text{nonvertical}:
\quad
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\rm fixed}.
\]

\[
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=1,\ \text{vertical}:
\quad
Y12\text{ is absorbed}.
\]

\[
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=2:
\quad
Y12\text{ is fully absorbed}.
\]

The necessary condition is

\[
\operatorname{dist}((0,1),\operatorname{ran}B_Y)\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

**Status:** critical blocker.

Graph-like lower connection is nonfatal. Vertical freedom is fatal.

---

## D. \(\mathrm{NoLocalFreeAmplitude}_{Y12}\)

Classify the coherent \(Y12\)-amplitude space

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Allowed:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0,
\]

fixed basepoint shifts,

finite-dimensional coherent amplitudes,

residual-controlled terms.

Fatal:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

contains extraction-scale local basis functions or otherwise arbitrary local \(Y12\)-amplitudes.

**Status:** critical blocker.

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\neq0\) but finite-dimensional/coherent, the downstream contradiction becomes block/global Schur rather than pointwise atomic.

---

## E. \(\mathrm{EffectiveWallLowOrder}\)

After graph subtraction,

\[
\ell_{\raw}
=
(\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
=
\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

Need

\[
\rho_\Gamma
=
\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}
\in\mathcal A_{\low},
\]

and

\[
\partial_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\rho_\Gamma=0,
\qquad
\partial_{q^{(4)}}\rho_\Gamma=0.
\]

Known/expected:

\[
c_7,\alpha_9\in\mathcal A_{\low}.
\]

Still to audit:

\[
\beta_9,\rho_Y\in\mathcal A_{\low}.
\]

**Status:** critical blocker.

If \(\rho_\Gamma\) depends on actual \(s_9^Y\), \(q^{(4)}\), or \(\Gamma_{G,2}\), the wall may be tautological.

---

## F. \(\mathrm{WallSchurFiniteType}\)

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0,
\]

the wall theorem is finite type for

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G.
\]

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\neq0,
\]

the theorem is

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
\left(
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G),
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\right)^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)\|^2.
\]

Failure means trapping near a forced D4-wall ODE:

\[
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+\rho_\Gamma)q''
-36\rho_\Gamma q_0
+12\rho_\Gamma q_0^3
-
6\frac{\phi}{H\alpha_9},
\qquad
\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

**Status:** critical after A–E.

If the forced ODE trap persists, either prove an anti-ODE theorem or route to a higher-order/rank-two residual.

---

## G. \(d_0\) computation

Only after

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_{\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}})[H\ell_{\raw}]
\]

is known, compute

\[
d_{\Gamma,\eff}
\]

and then

\[
d_0=d_N+2d_H+2d_{\Gamma,\eff}.
\]

**Status:** gates O2/O3 finite block.

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) projection has high branch-charge complexity, bounded-charge O2/O3 may break.

---

## H. Transition \(\mathbb C_{d_0}[y]\) visibility

Central fold coordinate:

\[
x=\frac12+\delta y.
\]

Need transition chart to retain

\[
\mathbb C_{d_0}[y]
\]

with \(R^C\)-conditioning.

If \(d_0\) is not finalized, use conservative

\[
d_{\max}
\]

such as \(14\), assuming it covers all plausible wall cases.

**Status:** important, can be handled conditionally with \(d_{\max}\).

---

## I. \(\mathrm{CanonicalQuadraticMultiplier}\)

Need pure-branch graph non-polynomiality:

\[
\operatorname{dist}(1/m_{\can,+},\mathbb C_R[x])\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Sufficient structure:

\[
m_{\can,+}(x)=\kappa(x-z_0)^2u(x)
\]

with \(z_0\) polynomially close and \(u\) nonvanishing/controlled.

**Status:** critical for fixed-\(\alpha\) O2/O3.

---

## J. Christoffel substitution spread

The substitution marginal should satisfy

\[
d\mu_{\sub}(x)=\frac1nK_n(x,x)w(x)\,dx,
\]

with

\[
\mu_{\sub}(J)\le R^C|J|
\]

and polynomial lower support.

**Status:** needed after I.

---

## K. \(\mathrm{NearPureMomentSeparation}\)

Use

\[
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

Need

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\mu_{\sub})}
\left(
1,
\operatorname{span}
\{R_{\fold},R_{\fold}^2,\ldots,R_{\fold}^{d_0}\}
\right)^2
\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

**Status:** finite after G, J.

---

## L. \(\mathrm{Agg}L^2\)

Gate–1 is:

\[
\mathrm{ThickObs}
+
\mathrm{MultiscaleFoldRouting}
+
\mathrm{FiniteAFEAntiLock}
+
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{L^2}.
\]

**Status:** upstream parallel block.

---

## M. Atomic/block contradiction

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0
\]

or block-local, use Type-I atomic contradiction.

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is global finite-dimensional, the contradiction becomes a block/global Schur-energy contradiction:

\[
\sum_\omega
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
F_Y|_\omega,
\mathcal C_{Y,\omega}^{\arith}
\right)^2.
\]

**Status:** final assembly.

---

# 2. Blockers and non-blockers

## Critical blockers

\[
A/B/C
\]

if \(Y12\) dies or cannot be coordinatized.

\[
D
\]

if amplitude is locally free.

\[
E
\]

if \(\rho_\Gamma\) is tautological or high-order.

\[
F
\]

if wall finite type fails and ODE trap is unresolved.

\[
I
\]

if pure-branch O2/O3 graph non-polynomiality fails.

## Nonfatal complications

Graph-like lower connection:

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\rm fixed}.
\]

Fixed \(E_2^Y\) basepoint.

Finite-dimensional coherent amplitude space.

Central fold concentration, if transition retains \(\mathbb C_{d_0}[y]\).

\(\beta_9\neq0\), as long as \(\rho_\Gamma\) is low-order and wall finite type holds.

---

# 3. Failure-type taxonomy

## Arithmetic absorption

\(Y12\) is genuine actual-source curvature:

\[
(0,1)\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\]

or \(Y12\in \mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\).

Then current \(Y12\) route dies conceptually.

## Nuisance overcollapse

Singular quotient kills the refined origin-pair label.

Potentially repairable by labelled realization or finer quotient.

## Labelled mismatch

Labelled transition does not represent actual singular rows modulo safe residual.

Need raw Schur test or new transition model.

## Amplitude absorption

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

absorbs

\[
H\ell_{\raw}.
\]

If finite-dimensional, use wall Schur. If locally free, current route dies.

## Wall ODE trap

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G
\]

is finite-type flat or forced by a coherent amplitude \(\phi\).

Need anti-ODE theorem or fallback.

## O2/O3 graph failure

\[
1/m_{\can,+}
\]

is too polynomial on active branch, killing pure-plus determinant mass.

## Local-free \(E_2\)

\(E_2^Y\) supplies extraction-scale arbitrary amplitude.

Fatal for \(Y12\) channel.

---

\mathrm{LiveY12DualPair}
}
\]

\to
\boxed{
\mathrm{LabelledTransitionY12}
}

\to

}

}

\[

}
\]

\[
\to
\boxed{
\mathrm{AtomicContradiction}.
}
\]

---

# 5. Fallback paths

## If \(Y12\) fails by arithmetic absorption

Move to another primitive carrier/order:

\[
K_{11},
\]

another order-nine carrier, or higher D4.

## If \(Y12\) fails by nuisance overcollapse

Refine the singular quotient or use labelled expression trees.

## If \(E_2^Y\) is finite-dimensional but nonlocal

Use block/global Schur wall theorem.

## If \(E_2^Y\) is locally free

Current \(Y12\) route dies.

## If \(\rho_\Gamma\) is not low-order

Wall may be tautological. Need a different quotient/wall or higher-order residual.

## If wall ODE trap persists

Try:

1. higher-order derivative residual of the wall ODE;
2. rank-two D4 if available;
3. anti-ODE theorem for actual EF source;
4. route ODE branch into O2/O3 or Gate–1 contradiction.

---

# 6. Next-round priority recommendation

The next six tasks should focus in this order:

1. **LiveY12DualPair**: build \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\), verify Gram conditioning.

2. **LabelledTransitionY12**: prove labelled-realization consistency.

3. **Y12FiberGraphSchur**: classify \(B_Y\), compute \(\rho_Y\).

4. **NoLocalFreeAmplitude\(_{Y12}\)**: classify \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\).

5. **EffectiveWallLowOrder**: certify \(\rho_\Gamma\in\mathcal A_{\low}\).

6. **WallSchurFiniteType**: prove wall finite type modulo \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\), or isolate the forced ODE trap.

Only after these are resolved should we finalize \(d_0\) and attack the exact O2/O3 near-pure block.

The current strategic sentence is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Fiber normality is necessary but not sufficient; amplitude non-absorption is also necessary.}
}
\]

That should govern the next coordinator round.

## Assistant (2026-04-28 06:08:58)

I’ll continue by probing the weakest link in the current dependency graph: if \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is finite-dimensional but global, the contradiction is no longer pointwise atomic. We need a block-level formulation that still avoids cancellation.

## Agent 6 continued attack — block Schur contradiction when \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\neq0\)

The cleanest downstream case is

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0,
\]

because then the wall remains pointwise/atomic:

\[
F_Y(p)=H(p)\alpha_9(p)(\Gamma_{G,2}(p)+\rho_\Gamma(p)\Gamma_G(p)).
\]

But the current architecture allows a more general case:

\[
0<\dim \mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\le Q^C.
\]

Then we cannot simply use pointwise atom lower bounds. We must use a **block Schur energy** contradiction.

---

# 1. Why pointwise atomicity can fail

If

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_{\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}})F_Y,
\]

then

\[
F_Y^{\eff}(p)
\]

may depend on all atoms in the projection block. If the projection is global, then a local D4 certificate at one atom does not automatically imply a pointwise lower bound at that same atom after projection.

Thus the old pointwise step

\[
|F_Y(p)|^2\ge Q^{-A}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
|F_Y^{\eff}(p)|^2\ge Q^{-A}
\]

is false.

Instead, the lower must be formulated as

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
(F_Y,\mathcal C_Y^{\arith})^2
\ge Q^{-A}\nu(\mathcal G).
}
\]

This is a global or block-level Schur complement.

---

# 2. Correct block formulation

Partition the smooth routed set into blocks

\[
\Omega
\]

with bounded overlap and polynomial mass. For each block define

\[
\mathcal C_{Y,\Omega}^{\arith}
=
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}|_\Omega.
\]

Define

\[
F_{Y,\Omega}
=
F_Y|_\Omega.
\]

The wall lower should be

\[
\boxed{
\sum_\Omega
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu_\Omega)}
\left(
F_{Y,\Omega},
\mathcal C_{Y,\Omega}^{\arith}
\right)^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\sum_\Omega
\|F_{Y,\Omega}\|_{L^2(\nu_\Omega)}^2.
}
\]

This remains Type-I if:

1. blocks have bounded overlap;
2. the coherent space restricts locally;
3. the projection is made before hard extraction;
4. each block receives only polynomially many D4 certificates.

So the atomic contradiction becomes a **block atomic contradiction**.

---

# 3. Block-size tradeoff

Block size must balance two constraints.

## Too small

If blocks are too small, a finite-dimensional coherent space may look locally free. For example, on a block with \(L\le M\) atoms,

\[
\mathcal C_{Y,\Omega}^{\arith}
\]

could interpolate any wall amplitude on that block.

Then

\[
\operatorname{dist}(F_{Y,\Omega},\mathcal C_{Y,\Omega}^{\arith})=0
\]

for trivial dimensional reasons.

## Too large

If blocks are too large, the block lower becomes too nonlocal, and extraction/mobility may lose locality.

The correct scale is:

\[
\boxed{
\#\Omega \gg \dim \mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
}
\]

but still polynomially local relative to chart and branch scales.

A sufficient condition is:

\[
\#\Omega \ge Q^A M,
\qquad
M=\dim\mathcal C_Y^{\arith},
\]

with block overlap \(Q^C\).

---

# 4. Block Schur complement

Let

\[
\Phi_\Omega
\]

be the matrix of coherent amplitudes on \(\Omega\). Then

\[
\delta_{\Omega}^2
=
\|F_{Y,\Omega}\|^2
-
F_{Y,\Omega}^*
\Phi_\Omega
(\Phi_\Omega^*\Phi_\Omega)^\dagger
\Phi_\Omega^*
F_{Y,\Omega}.
\]

If \(\Phi_\Omega^*\Phi_\Omega\) is nonsingular,

\[
\delta_\Omega^2
=
\frac{\det G_{\Phi_\Omega,F_{Y,\Omega}}}{\det G_{\Phi_\Omega}}.
\]

The theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\sum_\Omega \delta_\Omega^2
\ge Q^{-A}\sum_\Omega\|F_{Y,\Omega}\|^2.
}
\]

This is the exact amplitude Schur theorem.

---

# 5. Finite-type proof modulo \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\)

If the coherent space is spanned by low-complexity functions

\[
\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_M,
\]

then the Schur lower follows if no linear combination

\[
\phi=\sum a_j\phi_j
\]

can approximate

\[
F_Y
\]

too well on many blocks.

Equivalently, for every normalized \(\phi\in\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\),

\[
F_Y-\phi
\]

has polynomial finite type on a polynomial fraction of each relevant block.

In formulas:

\[
\max_{0\le j\le J}
\left|
\partial_m^j(F_Y-\phi)
\right|
\ge Q^{-C}\|F_Y-\phi\|_{\mathrm{scale}}
\]

on controlled subblocks.

This yields a Remez/sublevel bound:

\[
\|F_Y-\phi\|_{L^2(\Omega)}
\ge Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\Omega)}
\]

uniformly over \(\phi\).

This is the wall finite-type theorem modulo coherent amplitudes.

---

# 6. Forced ODE interpretation on blocks

If

\[
F_Y\approx \phi
\]

on a block, then

\[
q_{\can}
\]

approximately satisfies

\[
q^{(4)}
=
6(1-q_0^2+\rho_\Gamma)q''
-36\rho_\Gamma q_0
+12\rho_\Gamma q_0^3
-
6\frac{\phi}{H\alpha_9}.
\]

Thus each coherent amplitude \(\phi\) gives a forced ODE model.

The block Schur theorem is equivalent to:

\[
\boxed{
q_{\can}
\text{ is not locally trapped near the finite family of forced D4-wall ODE models generated by }\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
}
\]

This may be easier to prove if \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) has low dimension and low derivative complexity.

---

# 7. Interaction with O2/O3

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\neq0
\]

and projection is blockwise, the pre-extraction O2/O3 weight should be block-residual energy:

\[
W_{\bd,\Omega}
=
N_{\sm}\,\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu_\Omega)}
\left(
F_{Y,\Omega},
\mathcal C_{Y,\Omega}^{\arith}
\right)^2.
\]

This is not a pointwise bounded-degree function unless the projection is local and low-degree.

Therefore, for O2/O3 bounded-charge survival, we strongly prefer:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0
\quad\text{or fixed basepoint only.}
}
\]

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is genuinely nonzero, the proof must choose:

1. prove O2/O3 before projecting \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\), using raw \(F_Y\), then handle amplitude Schur later; or  
2. show the block projection has bounded branch-charge structure; or  
3. use \(\alpha\)-averaged O2/O3 instead of fixed branch-charge O2/O3.

This is a major dependency.

---

# 8. Best ordering if \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\neq0\)

Do not build O2/O3 weight from

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
\]

unless its branch-charge degree is controlled.

Instead:

## Step 1

Use raw smooth wall

\[
|F_Y|^2
\]

for O2/O3 branch survival. This preserves bounded charge.

## Step 2

After O2/O3, apply amplitude Schur to show

\[
F_Y
\]

has residual mass modulo

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

## Step 3

Extract good blocks/atoms from the residual mass.

This avoids contaminating the O2/O3 weight with nonlocal projections.

The price is that O2/O3 is proved with a wall that might partly be coherent. But if the raw wall has mass and amplitude Schur later removes only \(Q^{-A}\) fraction, this is fine.

---

# 9. Revised contradiction theorem with nonzero \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\)

Assume:

1. Raw wall aggregate:

\[
\int |F_Y|^2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-A}\nu(\mathcal C).
\]

2. Amplitude Schur:

\[
\operatorname{dist}(F_Y,\mathcal C_Y^{\arith})^2
\ge Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|^2.
\]

3. Actual-row upper:

\[
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
\Pi_YJ_{\sing}(q''_{\can}),
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\right)^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu(\mathcal C).
\]

Then contradiction follows after block extraction.

This is cleaner than trying to define pointwise \(F_Y^{\eff}\) first.

---

# 10. Updated strategic implication

The downstream theorem splits into:

\[
\boxed{
\text{raw wall mass}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\text{amplitude Schur non-absorption}.
}
\]

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0,
\]

these coincide.

If not, keep them separate to preserve O2/O3 bounded-charge structure.

This is the key correction.

---

# 11. Current priority

The next most important question is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0\text{ / fixed / finite-dimensional / locally free?}
}
\]

If zero/fixed, proceed with pointwise moving-wall finite type and bounded-charge O2/O3.

If finite-dimensional, use raw wall for O2/O3 and add a separate amplitude Schur theorem before extraction.

If locally free, the \(Y12\) route fails.

This is now the branch point for the downstream proof design.
