## Agent 6 — WeightedPlainWallTransfer\(_{2Y}\) and scalar O2/O3 integration

Assume the rank-two local exactness theorem has closed:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

Then D4 gives

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\asymp_{Q^A}
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

The corrected downstream path is:

\[
\text{plain rank-two wall}
+
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
\Longrightarrow
\text{weighted wall mass}
\Longrightarrow
\text{scalar O2/O3}.
\]

---

# 1. Starting analytic wall lower

Agent 5’s analytic closure should produce, on routed Gate–1-good blocks,

\[
\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(I^*)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Equivalently, after square-summing over blocks,

\[
\sum_{\Omega}
\left(
\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(\Omega^*)}^2
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2(\Omega)}^2
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\mu(\mathcal C).
\]

This is still unweighted. O2/O3 needs smooth weighted mass:

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)
\,d\nu.
\]

So we need a transfer theorem.

---

# 2. WeightedPlainWallTransfer\(_{2Y}\)

The desired theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}.
}
\]

If Gate–1 and rank-two coercivity give

\[
\sum_{\Omega}
\left(
\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(\Omega^*)}^2
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2(\Omega)}^2
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\mu(\mathcal C),
\]

then, after selecting a polynomial-mass subcollection of routed blocks,

\[
\boxed{
\int
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)
\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A'}\nu(\mathcal C').
}
\]

A sufficient package is:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A}
\]

on selected blocks,

\[
N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}
\]

or at least has polynomial lower average on selected blocks,

and the \(I\subset I^*\) enlargement has bounded overlap and compatible smooth weights.

---

# 3. Block-selection if weights are not pointwise good

If \(N_{\sm}|H|^2\) is not uniformly bounded below, use a dyadic block selection.

Let

\[
W_H(\Omega):=
\operatorname{Avg}_{\Omega}\left(N_{\sm}|H|^2\right).
\]

Dyadically decompose blocks by

\[
W_H(\Omega)\asymp 2^{-k}.
\]

If the total unweighted wall mass is polynomial, then either:

1. the blocks with \(W_H(\Omega)\ge Q^{-A}\) carry polynomial wall mass, giving the transfer; or  
2. the wall mass is concentrated where \(N_{\sm}|H|^2\) is too small.

Case 2 must be disallowed by the construction of \(N_{\sm}\), by the \(H\)-nondegeneracy theorem, or by a separate weight-good extraction lemma.

So the transfer theorem needs the following explicit assumption:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Gate--1-good wall mass is not concentrated on }N_{\sm}|H|^2<Q^{-A}.
}
\]

This can be phrased as a weighted-good subset theorem:

\[
\sum_{\Omega:\,W_H(\Omega)\ge Q^{-A}}
\left(
\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(\Omega^*)}^2
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2(\Omega)}^2
\right)
\ge Q^{-A'}\mu(\mathcal C).
\]

Then weighted transfer follows.

---

# 4. Compatibility of \(I\) and \(I^*\)

RankTwoCoercivity uses

\[
\Gamma_G
\]

on \(I^*\), while the wall weight may be integrated on \(I\). This is harmless if:

1. the enlarged blocks \(I^*\) have bounded overlap;
2. \(N_{\sm}|H|^2\) varies polynomially between \(I\) and \(I^*\);
3. wall mass on \(I^*\) can be assigned to the associated central block \(I\) at polynomial loss.

If this is awkward, formulate the wall lower directly on enlarged blocks:

\[
\int_{I^*}
N_{\sm}|H|^2|\Gamma_G|^2
+
\int_I
N_{\sm}|H|^2|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2.
\]

Then use bounded overlap to return to the global integral.

---

# 5. Finite-family scalar selection

Once weighted plain wall mass is known:

\[
\int
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)
\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A}\nu(\mathcal C),
\]

select one fixed scalar component at the smooth aggregate stage:

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

such that

\[
\boxed{
\int
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2
\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A'}\nu(\mathcal C).
}
\]

This selection must be global or over a polynomial-mass block class before hard extraction. It must not be atomwise.

Then O2/O3 runs with

\[
\boxed{
W_\star
=
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
}
\]

---

# 6. Scalar O2/O3 bandwidth

For

\[
G_\star=\Gamma_G
\]

or

\[
G_\star=\Gamma_{G,2},
\]

the bandwidth is

\[
\boxed{
d_0^{(2)}
=
d_N+2d_H+2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}).
}
\]

Need

\[
\boxed{
d_0^{(2)}\le d_{\max}.
}
\]

Keep

\[
d_{\max}=14
\]

unless the branch-degree audit forces a larger fixed value.

The O2/O3 theorem should be:

For every scalar wall \(G\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}\) with

\[
d_N+2d_H+2d_G\le d_{\max},
\]

the near-pure moment separation survives under the smooth weight

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G|^2.
\]

The finite pure/fold test remains

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\mu_{\sub})}
\left(
1,\operatorname{span}\{R_{\fold},R_{\fold}^2,\ldots,R_{\fold}^{d_0}\}
\right)
\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

---

# 7. Finite coherent amplitude case

If a finite coherent vector amplitude model remains:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}\neq0,
\]

do not project before O2/O3. The correct order is:

\[
\text{raw plain wall}
\to
\text{weighted transfer}
\to
\text{scalar O2/O3}
\to
\text{vector amplitude Schur}.
\]

After O2/O3, prove

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu_{\bd})}
(F_{2Y},\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith})^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|F_{2Y}\|^2.
}
\]

If this fails, route to the forced pair branch, not to rank-one moving wall.

---

# 8. Forced pair fallback

If vector amplitude Schur fails, we get coherent forcing:

\[
E_1\approx \Phi_1,
\qquad
E_2\approx \Phi_2
\]

with linked coefficients. Then

\[
E_2-E_1''=-12q(q')^2
\]

gives

\[
q(q')^2\approx -\frac{1}{12}(\Phi_2-\Phi_1'').
\]

Equivalently, the forced pair branch is

\[
q''=6q-2q^3+\Phi_1,
\]

\[
q(q')^2=\Phi_3.
\]

The division-free compatibility residual is

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal P_{\mathrm{pair}}
=
q^3(\Phi_3')^2
-
\Phi_3
\left(
\Phi_3+2q^2(6q-2q^3+\Phi_1)
\right)^2.
}
\]

This is a secondary fallback only. The main route should first attempt vector amplitude Schur.

---

# 9. Do not use jet wall unless derivative channels are proved

If weighted transfer fails because coercivity needs derivative control, the fix is **not** to insert

\[
D\Gamma_G,\quad D^2\Gamma_G
\]

into the O2/O3 wall by fiat.

Valid fallback options:

1. prove \(\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}\);
2. prove \(\mathrm{DerivativeD4Channels}_{2Y}\);
3. prove a block-difference theorem showing neighboring legitimate D4 observations control \(D\Gamma_G,D^2\Gamma_G\);
4. use a stronger completion/regularity theorem for \(\Gamma_G\).

Until then, the honest wall remains

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
\]

---

# 10. Final theorem statement

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Assume:

1. \(B_Y=0\), so D4 supplies the plain vector wall.
2. \(\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}\) and \(\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}\) give unweighted block wall mass.
3. \(N_{\sm}|H|^2\) is polynomially nondegenerate on a selected Gate–1-good block family.
4. \(I\subset I^*\) block enlargement has bounded overlap and weight compatibility.

Then

\[
\int
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)
\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A}\nu(\mathcal C').
\]

Then one fixed

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

supports scalar O2/O3 with

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
\]

This closes the rank-two wall-to-O2/O3 interface without introducing derivative walls.

## Agent 6 continued attack — weighted transfer reduces to an \(H\)-wall compatibility theorem

The corrected rank-two stack is now:

\[
B_Y=0
\Rightarrow
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}),
\]

then

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
\Rightarrow
\text{plain unweighted wall mass},
\]

then

\[
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}
\Rightarrow
\text{weighted wall mass for O2/O3}.
\]

The remaining transfer issue is whether the unweighted wall mass can concentrate where the O2/O3 prefactor

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\]

is too small.

So the next target is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{rank-two wall mass is not concentrated on the }H\text{-small or }N_{\sm}\text{-small set.}
}
\]

---

# 1. The precise weighted-transfer obstruction

Let

\[
W_2(m):=|\Gamma_G(m)|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}(m)|^2.
\]

From Gate–1/coercivity we expect, on the routed block family,

\[
\int W_2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-A}\nu(\mathcal C).
\]

O2/O3 needs

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-A'}\nu(\mathcal C).
\]

The only obstruction is concentration:

\[
W_2\,d\nu
\]

could concentrate where

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2\ll Q^{-A'}.
\]

Thus a necessary transfer lemma is:

\[
\boxed{
\int_{\{N_{\sm}|H|^2\ge Q^{-C}\}}W_2\,d\nu
\ge
Q^{-C'}
\int W_2\,d\nu.
}
\]

This is the cleanest form.

---

# 2. Split \(N_{\sm}\) and \(H\)

The factor

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\]

has two roles.

## \(N_{\sm}\)

This is a smooth extraction/normalization factor. It should be deliberately constructed to avoid killing Gate–1-good mass. If it can be arbitrarily small on good mass, the extraction scheme is wrong.

So require:

\[
\boxed{
\int_{\{N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-C}\}}W_2\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-C'}\int W_2\,d\nu.
}
\]

This is mostly a design condition for the smooth weight.

## \(H\)

This is structural. If

\[
H=0
\]

on the wall-mass region, then D4 amplitude is zero even though the curvature wall exists. Thus we need an \(H\)-anti-concentration theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\int_{\{|H|\ge Q^{-C}\}}W_2\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-C'}\int W_2\,d\nu.
}
\]

So the real mathematical input is

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{HWallCompatibility}_{2Y}.
}
\]

---

# 3. \(H\)-wall compatibility

The theorem should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{HWallCompatibility}_{2Y}:
\quad
H\text{ is not systematically small on rank-two wall mass.}
}
\]

Formally:

\[
\int |H|^2W_2\,d\nu
\ge
Q^{-A}
\int W_2\,d\nu.
\]

Equivalently, dyadic decomposition of \(|H|\) gives a dyadic band

\[
|H|\asymp Q^{-C_H}
\]

with polynomial wall mass.

If \(H\) is a known carrier coefficient such as a Gram/whitening determinant factor, this should follow from chart-goodness:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A}
\]

on the chart-good set.

If \(H\) has zeros, need sublevel control:

\[
\nu(|H|<\varepsilon;\ W_2\text{-weighted})
\le Q^C\varepsilon^\theta \nu.
\]

So the precise route depends on whether \(H\) is bounded away from zero by chart definition or merely anti-concentrated.

---

# 4. If \(H\) is a denominator already in chart-goodness

Best case:

\[
H
\]

is a product of Gram/normalization denominators already required to be good for D4. Then define the rank-two D4 chart by:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Then

\[
\int |H|^2W_2\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-2A}\int W_2\,d\nu
\]

immediately.

This would make \(H\)-compatibility automatic.

Then weighted transfer reduces to \(N_{\sm}\)-compatibility, which should be an extraction design lemma.

---

# 5. If \(H\) can vanish on chart-good sets

Then \(H\)-wall compatibility becomes a finite-model nonabsorption problem.

Need to rule out:

\[
H\approx0
\quad\text{and}\quad
(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})\text{ large}
\]

on most wall mass.

A sufficient condition is finite-type sublevel for \(H\) under wall-weighted measure:

\[
\int_{\{|H|<\varepsilon\}}W_2\,d\nu
\le Q^C\varepsilon^\theta\int W_2\,d\nu.
\]

This is again a finite model Riesz/control theorem. If \(H\) is low-order Gram algebra and \(W_2\) is finite-wall energy, then such sublevel should be more tractable than the rank-one moving-line problem.

---

# 6. Weighted-transfer theorem

A complete transfer theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Assume:

1. unweighted rank-two wall mass:

\[
\int W_2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-A}\nu(\mathcal C);
\]

2. \(H\)-wall compatibility:

\[
\int |H|^2W_2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-A_H}\int W_2\,d\nu;
\]

3. \(N_{\sm}\)-wall compatibility:

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A_N}\int |H|^2W_2\,d\nu.
\]

Then

\[
\boxed{
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A'}\nu(\mathcal C).
}
\]

This is the exact bridge into O2/O3.

---

# 7. Scalar component selection after weighting

Once weighted wall mass is known,

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A}\nu(\mathcal C),
\]

choose a fixed scalar component:

\[
G_\star=\Gamma_G
\quad\text{or}\quad
G_\star=\Gamma_{G,2}
\]

such that

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A'}\nu(\mathcal C).
\]

Then O2/O3 runs with

\[
W_\star=N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
\]

This selection is global or on a pre-extraction block class. It is not atomwise.

---

# 8. If \(H\)-compatibility fails

If

\[
\int |H|^2W_2
\]

is small despite large

\[
\int W_2,
\]

then D4 amplitude is suppressed exactly where the rank-two curvature wall lives.

This creates a new failure mode:

\[
\boxed{
\text{\(H\)-zero shielding of rank-two D4.}
}
\]

Possible responses:

## 8.1 Refine the D4 chart

Declare \(H\)-small regions outside the D4 chart and show Gate–1 mass routes elsewhere.

## 8.2 Use another D4 normalization

If \(H\) is an artifact of carrier normalization, rescale the carrier to absorb \(H\), provided this does not introduce singular denominators.

## 8.3 Treat \(H=0\) as a new wall

If \(H\)-smallness is structural, study the simultaneous model:

\[
H\approx0,\qquad \Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\text{ large}.
\]

This might produce another finite-model contradiction, but it is a separate branch.

Best is to avoid this by chart-goodness.

---

# 9. Current theorem stack with transfer included

The corrected rank-two stack becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{LiveFiberAvoidance}_{2Y}:B_Y=0
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4VectorWall}:F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
\Rightarrow
\int W_2\ge Q^{-A}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{HWallCompatibility}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{NSmoothCompatibility}
\Rightarrow
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\ge Q^{-A'}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{select }G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{O2/O3}\text{ with }N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
}
\]

---

# 10. Current conclusion

The weighted-transfer bridge exposes one more precise needed theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{HWallCompatibility}_{2Y}.
}
\]

If \(H\) is already bounded below on the D4 chart, this is automatic. If not, it becomes the next finite-model sublevel problem.

So the rank-two route now has three honest nonlocal inputs after local exactness:

1. \(\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}\);
2. \(\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}\);
3. \(\mathrm{HWallCompatibility}_{2Y}\).

All are much cleaner than the rank-one moving-wall/trap tower.

## Agent 6 continued attack — \(H\)-compatibility should be absorbed into the D4 chart if possible

The rank-two route now has a precise weighted-transfer requirement:

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)d\nu
\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

The unweighted wall lower comes from

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}.
\]

So the only remaining transfer obstruction is concentration of wall mass where

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\]

is too small.

I think the correct strategy is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{make }|H|\ge Q^{-A}\text{ part of the D4 chart-good condition if possible.}
}
\]

If this works, \(H\)-compatibility disappears as a separate theorem.

---

# 1. \(H\) should be treated as a D4 coefficient denominator

The rank-two D4 wall is

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

For this to be a meaningful D4 lower, the coefficient matrix and scalar prefactor must be polynomially conditioned:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9|\le Q^A.
\]

We already require coefficient conditioning for

\[
c_7,\alpha_9,\beta_9.
\]

So \(H\) should be included in the same chart-good package:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGood}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Then

\[
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2)
\]

and

\[
|H|^2(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2)
\ge
Q^{-A}(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2).
\]

Thus \(H\)-weighted transfer is automatic on D4-good blocks.

---

# 2. What if \(H\)-good blocks do not carry Gate–1 mass?

Then the rank-two D4 route fails for a specific reason:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Gate--1 wall mass is routed into }H\text{-degenerate D4 charts.}
}
\]

This is not a wall anti-concentration problem. It is a chart-routing problem.

The necessary theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}.
}
\]

It should say that a polynomial fraction of Gate–1-good routed mass lies in charts where

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A}
\]

and the D4 coefficient matrix is conditioned.

If not, then the proof must either:

1. choose a different D4 chart/normalization;
2. use another carrier whose prefactor does not vanish;
3. prove that \(H\)-degenerate charts themselves imply a contradiction or route to another wall.

So \(H\)-compatibility should be handled at the **routing/chart selection** layer, not late in O2/O3.

---

# 3. \(N_{\sm}\)-compatibility is an extraction design condition

The other factor is

\[
N_{\sm}.
\]

Unlike \(H\), \(N_{\sm}\) is chosen by the proof. It should not be allowed to eliminate wall mass.

Thus require:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NSmoothGood}_{2Y}:
\quad
\int N_{\sm} W_2\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A}\int W_2\,d\nu
}
\]

on D4-good routed blocks, where

\[
W_2=|H|^2(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2).
\]

If \(N_{\sm}\) is a smooth cutoff for routed good regions, this should be built into its construction. It is not a new arithmetic theorem.

---

# 4. Revised weighted-transfer architecture

The clean architecture is:

## Step 1 — D4-good routing

Select a routed subcollection

\[
\mathcal C_{\D4}
\]

where

\[
|H|,\ |c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9|

are polynomially controlled.

\mu(\mathcal C_{\D4})\ge Q^{-A}\mu(\mathcal C)

or at least Gate–1 wall mass remains polynomial there.

## Step 2 — rank-two coercive wall lower on \(\mathcal C_{\D4}\)

Use Gate–1 and coercivity to get

\[
\int_{\mathcal C_{\D4}}
(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2)\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A}\mu(\mathcal C_{\D4}).
\]

## Step 3 — coefficient weighting

Since \(|H|\ge Q^{-A}\),

\[
\int_{\mathcal C_{\D4}}
|H|^2(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2)\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A'}
\mu(\mathcal C_{\D4}).
\]

## Step 4 — smooth extraction weight

Choose \(N_{\sm}\) with polynomial lower average on \(\mathcal C_{\D4}\), giving

\[
\int_{\mathcal C_{\D4}}
N_{\sm}|H|^2(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2)\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A''}\mu(\mathcal C_{\D4}).
\]

## Step 5 — scalar component selection

Choose

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

at smooth aggregate stage.

Then run O2/O3 with

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
\]

\int
(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2)d\nu.

to D4 chart-goodness and prove routing.

# 1. The \(H\)-classification fork

There are three possible meanings of \(H\).

## Case A: \(H\) is a coefficient/normalization denominator

Here \(H\) comes from Gram, whitening, carrier normalization, chart scale, or determinant normalization.

Then \(H\)-smallness means the D4 coordinate system is ill-conditioned.

Correct response:

\[
\boxed{
|H|\ge Q^{-A}\text{ belongs to D4CoeffGood}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Then rank-two D4 is only claimed on charts where the D4 extractor is conditioned.

This is the best case.

## Case B: \(H\) is a finite low-complexity amplitude

Here \(H\) is not purely a denominator; it can vanish dynamically but belongs to a low-complexity finite model.

Then need:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{HNonAbsorption}_{2Y}
}
\]

or wall-weighted sublevel control:

\[
\int_{\{|H|<\varepsilon\}}
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu
\le
Q^C\varepsilon^\theta
\int
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu.
\]

## Case C: \(H\) is locally adjustable/free

Then \(H\) can suppress D4 exactly where the wall lives.

That is fatal for this carrier normalization unless we:

1. renormalize the D4 carrier to remove \(H\);
2. choose another D4 chart;
3. use a different carrier whose prefactor is not locally free.

This would be a serious local-amplitude obstruction.

---

# 2. Why Case A is likely

The D4 matrix already contains coefficients

\[
c_7,\quad \beta_9,\quad \alpha_9.
\]

We require

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0
\]

and polynomial conditioning:

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9|
\le Q^A.
\]

The scalar \(H\) appears multiplicatively with the whole D4 output, so it is likely a common carrier normalization or geometric determinant factor.

Such a factor should be treated consistently with \(c_7,\alpha_9,\beta_9\):

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGood}_{2Y}:
|H|,\ |H|^{-1},\ |c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9|
\le Q^A.
}
\]

If this is the right interpretation, \(H\)-compatibility is not an analytic wall theorem. It is part of chart routing.

---

# 3. D4CoeffGoodRouting

The routing theorem should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}.
}
\]

On a polynomial fraction of Gate–1-good routed mass, the D4 coefficient package is good:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
|c_7|,|c_7^{-1}|,|\alpha_9|,|\alpha_9^{-1}|,|\beta_9|\le Q^A.
\]

More precisely, if Gate–1 gives mass on \(\mathcal C\), then there is a subcollection

\[
\mathcal C_{\D4}\subset\mathcal C
\]

with

\[
\mu(\mathcal C_{\D4})\ge Q^{-A}\mu(\mathcal C)
\]

or at least carrying polynomial Gate–1 energy, such that D4CoeffGood holds on \(\mathcal C_{\D4}\).

Then rank-two D4 is claimed only on \(\mathcal C_{\D4}\).

---

# 4. How to prove D4CoeffGoodRouting

Use dyadic decomposition of each coefficient.

For \(H\), decompose by

\[
|H|\asymp 2^{-k}.
\]

If \(H\) is a chart denominator, the atlas should already exclude bands with

\[
|H|<Q^{-A}.
\]

If exclusion loses too much Gate–1 mass, then the chart system is incomplete and must route those points to a different D4 chart.

So the theorem is really an atlas coverage statement:

\[
\boxed{
\text{D4-good charts cover polynomial Gate--1 mass.}
}
\]

This is analogous to ordinary nondegenerate-coordinate routing.

---

# 5. Weighted transfer after D4CoeffGood

On \(\mathcal C_{\D4}\),

\[
|H|^2\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Thus unweighted rank-two wall mass

\[
\int_{\mathcal C_{\D4}}
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu
\ge Q^{-A}
\]

implies

\[
\int_{\mathcal C_{\D4}}
|H|^2
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu
\ge Q^{-A'}.
\]

Then \(N_{\sm}\)-compatibility gives

\[
\int_{\mathcal C_{\D4}}
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu
\ge Q^{-A''}.
\]

So \(H\)-transfer becomes automatic once D4CoeffGoodRouting is available.

---

# 6. \(N_{\sm}\) should be chosen after D4 chart routing

The smooth weight

\[
N_{\sm}
\]

should be supported or lower-bounded on the D4-good routed family.

Correct sequence:

1. Gate–1 gives source energy.
2. D4CoeffGoodRouting selects \(\mathcal C_{\D4}\).
3. Define or restrict \(N_{\sm}\) so that

\[
N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}
\]

on a polynomial fraction of \(\mathcal C_{\D4}\).

4. Prove weighted wall mass.
5. Select scalar component \(G_\star\).

Do not choose \(N_{\sm}\) before knowing which D4 chart branch is used if that risks cutting away D4-good mass.

---

# 7. If \(H\)-small mass is unavoidable

Suppose Gate–1 mass lies mostly where

\[
|H|<Q^{-A}.
\]

Then rank-two D4 with this normalization cannot provide a lower there. But this gives a concrete fallback branch:

\[
\boxed{
H\text{-degenerate branch}.
}
\]

This branch should be analyzed separately.

Possible outcomes:

## 7.1 Alternate D4 chart

The vanishing of \(H\) indicates a bad coordinate patch, and another chart has a nonzero prefactor.

## 7.2 Structural degeneracy

The condition \(H=0\) is a finite algebraic relation in Gram/whitening variables. It may force another obstruction or simplify O2/O3.

## 7.3 Genuine no-go

If all D4 charts have common prefactor degeneration on Gate–1 mass, rank-two D4 route fails.

Thus \(H\)-smallness is not something to hide in weighted transfer; it is a branch of the D4 atlas.

---

This keeps \(H\) out of late-stage mystery assumptions.

plus

## Agent 6 — \( \mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y} \) and \( \mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R} \)

Assume the local exactness branch has closed:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

Then

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

On coefficient-good charts this is polynomially equivalent to

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

The remaining routing question is whether enough Gate–1/rank-two wall mass lives where the D4 coefficient package and smooth O2/O3 weight are nondegenerate.

---

# 1. \(H\)-classification

The scalar prefactor \(H\) must be classified before weighted transfer.

## Case A — coefficient / normalization denominator

If \(H\) is a Gram, whitening, chart, determinant, or carrier-normalization factor, then it belongs in the D4 chart-good condition:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

This is the preferred interpretation.

Then \(H\)-smallness means the chosen D4 coordinate chart is bad, not that the wall has analytically failed.

## Case B — finite low-complexity amplitude

If \(H\) is a dynamic finite-model amplitude, then one needs an \(H\)-wall nonabsorption theorem:

\[
\int_{\{|H|<\varepsilon\}}
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu
\le
Q^C\varepsilon^\theta
\int
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu.
\]

This is a fallback.

## Case C — locally adjustable/free amplitude

If \(H\) can be locally fitted, it can suppress D4 exactly where the wall lives. Then this normalization is unusable for the rank-two route unless one renormalizes, switches D4 chart, or uses a different carrier.

---

# 2. \( \mathrm{D4CoeffGood}_{2Y} \)

Define the coefficient-good condition:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGood}_{2Y}
}
\]

by

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9|
\le Q^A.
\]

On this set,

\[
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right),
\]

and also

\[
|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)
\ge
Q^{-A}
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right).
\]

---

# 3. \( \mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y} \)

The routing theorem should say:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Let \(\mathcal C\) be the Gate–1/routed block family. There exists a subfamily

\[
\mathcal C_{\D4}\subseteq \mathcal C
\]

such that:

\[
\mathrm{D4CoeffGood}_{2Y}
\]

holds on \(\mathcal C_{\D4}\), and

\[
\sum_{\Omega\subset \mathcal C_{\D4}}
\int_\Omega
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)d\nu
\ge
Q^{-A}
\sum_{\Omega\subset \mathcal C}
\int_\Omega
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)d\nu.
\]

In words: a polynomial portion of the rank-two wall mass lies on D4-coefficient-good charts.

If this fails, the route enters an \(H\)-degenerate branch:

\[
|H|\ll Q^{-A}
\]

on most wall mass. That must be routed to:

1. an alternate D4 chart;
2. a renormalization that removes \(H\);
3. an \(H\)-sublevel finite-model theorem;
4. or a no-go for this D4 carrier normalization.

---

# 4. Weighted transfer setup

Let

\[
W_2:=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2,
\]

and

\[
\Omega_{\sm}:=N_{\sm}|H|^2.
\]

After \(\mathrm{PlainWallRankTwoClosure}\), we have unweighted wall mass:

\[
\int W_2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

O2/O3 needs

\[
\int \Omega_{\sm} W_2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-A'}.
\]

So the exact transfer issue is:

\[
W_2\,d\nu
\]

must not concentrate where

\[
\Omega_{\sm}=N_{\sm}|H|^2
\]

is too small.

---

# 5. \(R^{-C}\)-scale good-weight sets

The good-weight set should be

\[
E_\Omega
=
\{m:\Omega_{\sm}(m)\ge Q^{-A}\}.
\]

The important scale correction is:

\[
\boxed{
|E_\Omega\cap I|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
}
\]

not merely

\[
Q^{-C}|I|.
\]

Reason: if \(W_2\) belongs to a finite algebra of effective degree \(R\), a Remez inequality typically loses powers like

\[
(|I|/|E|)^R.
\]

Thus \(R^{-C}\)-large sets preserve polynomial \(Q\)-scale bounds, while \(Q^{-C}\)-small sets may not.

---

# 6. Vector wall Remez

Need a vector Remez theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{VectorWallRemez}_{2Y}.
}
\]

For

\[
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2,
\]

if

\[
E\subset I,
\qquad
|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

then

\[
\boxed{
\int_E W_2\,d\nu
\ge
Q^{-C}
\int_I W_2\,d\nu.
}
\]

This follows if \(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\) lie in a controlled finite model/Remez class on \(I\), with polynomial \(R\)-scale constants.

This theorem is another instance of finite model Riesz/Remez control.

---

# 7. \( \mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R} \)

The transfer theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R}.
}
\]

Assume on a routed block family:

1. unweighted wall mass:

\[
\int_I W_2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-A}|I|;
\]

2. good-weight set:

\[
E_\Omega=\{\Omega_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}\}
\]

satisfies

\[
|E_\Omega\cap I|\ge R^{-C}|I|;
\]

3. vector wall Remez holds:

\[
\int_{E_\Omega\cap I} W_2\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2\,d\nu.
\]

Then

\[
\int_I \Omega_{\sm}W_2\,d\nu
\ge
Q^{-A-C}\int_I W_2\,d\nu
\ge
Q^{-A'}|I|.
\]

Global summation over selected blocks gives

\[
\boxed{
\int
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)d\nu
\ge
Q^{-A'}\nu(\mathcal C_{\D4}).
}
\]

---

# 8. Scalar component selection

From weighted plain-wall mass,

\[
\int
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)d\nu
\ge Q^{-A},
\]

select one fixed component at the smooth aggregate stage:

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

such that

\[
\boxed{
\int
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2\,d\nu
\ge
Q^{-A'}.
}
\]

This selection must be global, or at worst over a pre-extraction polynomial-mass block class. It must not be atomwise after extraction.

Then run scalar O2/O3 with

\[
\boxed{
W_\star=N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
}
\]

The bandwidth is

\[
\boxed{
d_0^{(2)}
=
d_N+2d_H+2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}).
}
\]

Need

\[
d_0^{(2)}\le d_{\max}.
\]

---

# 9. If transfer fails

## 9.1 \(H\)-degenerate branch

If wall mass lies where \(H\) is too small, classify \(H\). If \(H\) is a denominator, route to another chart. If \(H\) is a low-complexity amplitude, prove \(H\)-sublevel nonabsorption. If \(H\) is local/free, this carrier normalization fails.

## 9.2 \(N_{\sm}\)-small branch

If \(N_{\sm}\) cuts away wall mass, the smooth extraction is badly chosen. Adjust \(N_{\sm}\) or select a different routed good set.

## 9.3 Remez failure

If \(W_2\) can concentrate outside every \(R^{-C}\)-large weight-good set, then the finite wall class lacks Remez control. This is a finite-model regularity failure.

---

# 10. Final package

The combined theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R}
}
\]

with outputs:

\[
\int
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)d\nu
\ge Q^{-A},
\]

and then

\[
\exists\,G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

such that scalar O2/O3 applies to

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
\]

This bridges the corrected honest D4 plain wall into O2/O3 without introducing jet wall components.

The corrected rank-two route is now:

on D4-coefficient-good charts, then

\[

# 1. Predetermined good-weight set

Define

\[
\Omega_{\sm}:=N_{\sm}|H|^2.
\]

For each routed block \(I\), define the good-weight subset

\[
E_I:=\{m\in I:\Omega_{\sm}(m)\ge Q^{-A}\}.
\]

This set is allowed to depend on:

\[
H,\quad N_{\sm},\quad \text{chart data},\quad \text{coefficient conditioning}.
\]

It must not depend on:

\[
\Gamma_G,\quad \Gamma_{G,2},\quad W_2,
\]

except through pre-existing chart routing that is fixed before wall extraction.

Then require

\[
\boxed{
|E_I|\ge R^{-C}|I|
}
\]

for every selected block or for a polynomial-mass subfamily of blocks.

This is the correct non-circular weighted-transfer hypothesis.

---

# 2. Remez transfers wall mass onto predetermined \(E_I\)

Assume \(W_2\) belongs to a finite Remez class on \(I\). More precisely, assume for every predetermined measurable set \(E\subset I\) with

\[
|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

we have

\[
\boxed{
\int_E W_2
\ge Q^{-C}
\int_I W_2.
}
\]

Then with \(E=E_I\),

\[
\int_I \Omega_{\sm}W_2
\ge
\int_{E_I} \Omega_{\sm}W_2
\ge
Q^{-A}\int_{E_I}W_2
\ge
Q^{-A-C}\int_I W_2.
\]

Thus weighted transfer follows blockwise.

This avoids any need to show \(W_2\) is not concentrated on a wall-dependent set. The good set is predetermined by coefficients.

---

# 3. What Remez class is needed for \(W_2\)?

Since

\[
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2,
\]

it is enough to have vector Remez for the two-dimensional function

\[
\Gamma=(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

A useful sufficient statement is:

For every block \(I\), \(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\) lie in finite-dimensional spaces

\[
\Gamma_G\in \mathcal W_G(I),
\qquad
\Gamma_{G,2}\in\mathcal W_{G2}(I),
\]

with controlled Remez constants. Then the vector norm

\[
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\]

satisfies

\[
\int_E W_2
\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2
\]

for all

\[
|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

This can be proved by applying scalar Remez to whichever component carries at least half of the \(L^2(I)\) mass.

Indeed, if

\[
\int_I |\Gamma_G|^2\ge \frac12\int_I W_2,
\]

then scalar Remez for \(\Gamma_G\) gives

\[
\int_E |\Gamma_G|^2
\ge Q^{-C}\int_I |\Gamma_G|^2
\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2.
\]

Similarly for \(\Gamma_{G,2}\).

Thus vector Remez reduces to scalar Remez for each component.

---

# 4. Required finite spaces

For \(\Gamma_G\),

\[
\Gamma_G=-6q''+36q-12q^3,
\]

so use

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
D^2\mathcal V_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I.
\]

For \(\Gamma_{G,2}\),

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''
=
q^{(4)}-6q''+6q^2q''.
\]

Thus use

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^4\mathcal V_I+D^2\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot D^2\mathcal V_I.
\]

So the finite Remez package needs controlled Riesz/Remez constants for:

\[
D^2\mathcal V_I,\quad D^4\mathcal V_I,\quad \mathcal V_I,\quad \operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,\quad \operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot D^2\mathcal V_I.
\]

This is still a fixed-degree algebra of the local chart.

---

# 5. Relation to LocalMarkov

Notice that LocalMarkov for \(\Gamma_G\) used

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
D^2\mathcal V_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I.
\]

Weighted transfer needs Remez for both

\[
\mathcal W_G
\]

and

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}.
\]

So the finite-atlas analytic package should be enlarged slightly:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
}
\]

controlling:

1. Markov for \(\mathcal W_G\) from \(L^2(I^*)\) to \(H^2(I)\);
2. Remez for \(\mathcal W_G\);
3. Remez for \(\mathcal W_{G2}\).

This unifies LocalMarkov and WeightedPlainWallTransfer.

---

# 6. Potential issue: \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) includes \(q^{(4)}\)

The space \(\mathcal W_{G2}\) includes

\[
D^4\mathcal V_I.
\]

Thus the local chart must control fourth derivatives. This is already implicit in RankTwoCoercivity because

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}=E_2
\]

uses \(q^{(4)}\).

So no new derivative order is introduced by weighted transfer. But we must explicitly include \(D^4\mathcal V_I\) in the finite-atlas package.

If the local EF/AFE chart only has reliable control to second derivative, then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) itself is not a legitimate finite wall component. Since D4 gives \(\Gamma_{G,2}\), the chart must be upgraded.

---

# 7. Smooth aggregate selection protocol

The full non-circular protocol is:

1. Choose routed blocks \(I\) and finite chart spaces \(\mathcal V_I\).
2. Choose D4 coefficient-good blocks using only coefficient data:

\[
|H|,\ c_7,\alpha_9,\beta_9.
\]

3. Choose \(N_{\sm}\) and the good-weight set

\[
E_I=\{N_{\sm}|H|^2\ge Q^{-A}\}
\]

using only smooth chart/coefficient data.

4. Prove \(E_I\) is \(R^{-C}\)-large on selected blocks.

5. Prove Remez for \(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\) on every such \(I\).

6. Then transfer unweighted wall mass to weighted wall mass.

7. Only then choose scalar \(G_\star\) by aggregate pigeonhole.

This ordering avoids circularity.

---

# 8. If \(E_I\) is not \(R^{-C}\)-large

Then the smooth weight/cutoff is too restrictive on that block.

Possible fixes:

## 8.1 Enlarge \(N_{\sm}\)

If \(N_{\sm}\) was chosen too narrowly, broaden it.

## 8.2 Reroute D4 chart

If \(H\)-good set is small because \(H\) is small, choose another D4 chart or classify the \(H\)-degenerate branch.

## 8.3 Drop the block

If enough other blocks remain, discard it.

But if most Gate–1 mass lies in blocks where

\[
|E_I|<R^{-C}|I|,
\]

then the current chart/extraction atlas does not support rank-two O2/O3 transfer.

---

# 9. Updated theorem: WeightedPlainWallTransfer\(_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}}\)

The non-circular version:

Assume for a block family \(\mathcal B\):

1. unweighted wall mass:

\[
\sum_{I\in\mathcal B}\int_I W_2\ge Q^{-A}\mu(\mathcal B);
\]

2. predetermined good-weight sets

\[
E_I=\{N_{\sm}|H|^2\ge Q^{-A}\}
\]

satisfy

\[
|E_I|\ge R^{-C}|I|;
\]

3. scalar Remez holds for \(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\):

for every \(E\subset I\) with \(|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|\),

\[
\int_E |\Gamma_G|^2\ge Q^{-C}\int_I|\Gamma_G|^2,
\]

and similarly for \(\Gamma_{G,2}\).

Then

\[
\sum_{I\in\mathcal B}
\int_I
N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2
\ge
Q^{-A'}\mu(\mathcal B).
\]

This is the precise weighted transfer theorem.

---

# 10. Current conclusion

The weighted-transfer gate is not just “\(H\) not too small.”

It is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{predetermined \(N_{\sm}|H|^2\)-good sets of size }R^{-C}
+
\text{Remez for }\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}.
}
\]

This is another finite-atlas Riesz/Remez condition.

So the rank-two analytic package should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
}
\]

covering:

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G},
\]

\[
\mathrm{Remez}_{\Gamma_G},
\]

\[
\mathrm{Remez}_{\Gamma_{G,2}}.
\]

Then weighted transfer becomes clean and non-circular.

## Agent 6 continued attack — collapse LocalMarkov and weighted transfer into one atlas theorem

The current rank-two analytic side has split into:

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
\]

and

\[
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}}.
\]

But both are really consequences of the same finite-atlas regularity package. The right consolidated theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}.
}
\]

This should control the finite wall model spaces for

\[
\Gamma_G,\qquad \Gamma_{G,2},
\]

both for derivative Markov and for Remez transfer onto predetermined weight-good sets.

---

# 1. Define the finite atlas wall spaces

Let \(\mathcal V_I\) be the local finite EF/AFE chart on a routed block \(I\subset I^*\).

Define

\[
\mathcal W_G(I)
=
D^2\mathcal V_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I.
\]

Since

\[
\Gamma_G=-6q''+36q-12q^3,
\]

we have

\[
\Gamma_G\in\mathcal W_G(I).
\]

Define

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}(I)
=
D^4\mathcal V_I
+
D^2\mathcal V_I
+
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot D^2\mathcal V_I.
\]

Since

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}
=
q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''
=
q^{(4)}-6q''+6q^2q'',
\]

we have

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}\in\mathcal W_{G2}(I).
\]

So the analytic rank-two wall lives in the finite model pair

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal W_{2Y}(I)
=
\mathcal W_G(I)\oplus \mathcal W_{G2}(I).
}
\]

---

# 2. Required outputs of \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\)

The theorem should output three estimates.

## 2.1 Markov for \(\Gamma_G\)

For all

\[
w\in\mathcal W_G(I),
\]

\[
\boxed{
\|w\|_{H^2(I)}
\le
Q^C\|w\|_{L^2(I^*)}.
}
\]

This gives

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}.
\]

## 2.2 Remez for \(\Gamma_G\)

For every predetermined

\[
E\subset I
\]

with

\[
|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

and every

\[
w\in\mathcal W_G(I),
\]

\[
\boxed{
\int_E |w|^2
\ge
Q^{-C}
\int_I |w|^2.
}
\]

## 2.3 Remez for \(\Gamma_{G,2}\)

For every predetermined

\[
E\subset I
\]

with

\[
|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

and every

\[
w\in\mathcal W_{G2}(I),
\]

\[
\boxed{
\int_E |w|^2
\ge
Q^{-C}
\int_I |w|^2.
}
\]

Together, these imply vector Remez for

\[
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2.
\]

---

# 3. Why this theorem is plausible

All involved spaces are fixed-degree algebraic closures of \(\mathcal V_I\):

\[
D^2\mathcal V_I,\quad
D^4\mathcal V_I,\quad
\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,\quad
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot D^2\mathcal V_I.
\]

No unbounded product degree appears.

Thus, if \(\mathcal V_I\) has:

1. polynomial dimension;
2. polynomial Riesz constants on \(I\subset I^*\);
3. polynomial derivative Markov bounds up to order \(4\);
4. polynomial product/Remez stability through degree \(3\);

then \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) should follow.

This is the same finite-model control architecture already needed for O2/O3 and amplitude Schur.

---

# 4. Non-circular weighted transfer using FiniteAtlasRiesz

Let

\[
\Omega_{\sm}=N_{\sm}|H|^2.
\]

For each selected routed block \(I\), define the predetermined weight-good set

\[
E_I=\{m\in I:\Omega_{\sm}(m)\ge Q^{-A}\}.
\]

Assume:

\[
|E_I|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

This set depends only on chart/coefficient/smooth extraction data, not on

\[
\Gamma_G,\quad \Gamma_{G,2}.
\]

By \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\), if

\[
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2,
\]

then

\[
\int_{E_I}W_2
\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2.
\]

Therefore,

\[
\int_I \Omega_{\sm}W_2
\ge
\int_{E_I}\Omega_{\sm}W_2
\ge
Q^{-A}\int_{E_I}W_2
\ge
Q^{-A-C}\int_I W_2.
\]

So the weighted transfer follows without circularity.

---

# 5. RankTwoCoercivity after FiniteAtlasRiesz

Assume the honest D4 wall is small on \(I\):

\[
\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(I^*)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le \delta.
\]

By the Markov part of \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\),

\[
\|\Gamma_G\|_{H^2(I)}
\le Q^C\delta.
\]

Then RankTwoCoercivity gives

\[
\|q''\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^C\delta^\theta.
\]

Gate–1 gives

\[
\|q''\|_{L^2(I)}
\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Therefore

\[
\delta\ge Q^{-A'}.
\]

So the unweighted wall lower follows, and weighted transfer then follows from the Remez part.

Thus one atlas theorem feeds both steps.

---

# 6. The residual issue

If

\[
q
\]

is approximated by a finite chart source

\[
q_X\in\mathcal V_I
\]

with residual \(r\), we need

\[
\Gamma_G(q)
=
\Gamma_G(q_X)+R_G,
\]

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}(q)
=
\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)+R_{G2},
\]

with

\[
\|R_G\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|R_{G2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
\]

This is the rank-two wall completion:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}.
}
\]

It is narrower than a full singular completion; it controls only the two wall functions needed for rank-two closure.

If this fails, the analytic route must be stated for the finite model first and then extended by a residual theorem.

---

# 7. Updated analytic theorem stack

The rank-two analytic block should now be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{Gate1}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}}
}
\]

But the first theorem supplies both LocalMarkov and Remez transfer. So the true dependency list is shorter than before.

---

# 8. Interaction with scalar O2/O3

After weighted transfer, choose

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

at smooth aggregate stage.

The scalar O2/O3 wall weight is

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
\]

The bandwidth is

\[
d_0^{(2)}
=
d_N+2d_H+2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}).
\]

No derivative wall components are used. The derivatives entered only inside \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) and RankTwoCoercivity.

This preserves the correction that the honest D4 wall is plain.

---

# 9. What can break \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\)

## 9.1 High product leverage

\[
\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I
\]

or

\[
\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot D^2\mathcal V_I
\]

may have high leverage on \(I\). Then Remez/Markov fails.

## 9.2 Dimension too large

If \(\dim\mathcal V_I\) is too large, product spaces may exceed polynomial complexity.

## 9.3 No \(D^4\)-control

If \(D^4\mathcal V_I\) is not controlled, then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) itself lacks a finite model.

## 9.4 Bad block enlargement

If \(I^*\) crosses chart boundaries, the Markov estimate may fail.

These are finite-atlas failures, not D4-specific failures.

---

# 10. Current conclusion

The analytic side of the rank-two route is now best viewed as one finite-atlas theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
}
\]

controlling:

\[
\mathcal W_G=D^2\mathcal V+\mathcal V+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V,
\]

and

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}=D^4\mathcal V+D^2\mathcal V+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V\cdot D^2\mathcal V.
\]

It supplies both:

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
\]

and

\[
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}}.
\]

That is the cleanest consolidation.

## Agent 6 continued attack — finite-atlas failure modes and how they route

The rank-two analytic package has now consolidated to:

\[
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}.
\]

This supplies wall mass for the honest D4 wall:

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
\]

The next useful step is to classify what happens if \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) fails. This is important because it tells us whether failure is fatal or becomes another structured branch.

---

# 1. Restate the finite-atlas theorem

Let \(\mathcal V_I\) be the local source chart.

Define

\[
\mathcal W_G
=
D^2\mathcal V_I+\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I,
\]

because

\[
\Gamma_G=-6q''+36q-12q^3.
\]

Define

\[
\mathcal W_{G2}
=
D^4\mathcal V_I+D^2\mathcal V_I+\operatorname{Sym}^2\mathcal V_I\cdot D^2\mathcal V_I,
\]

because

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}
=
q^{(4)}-6q''+6q^2q''.
\]

\(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) should provide:

\[
\|w\|_{H^2(I)}
\le Q^C\|w\|_{L^2(I^*)}
\qquad(w\in\mathcal W_G),
\]

and Remez inequalities for both \(\mathcal W_G\) and \(\mathcal W_{G2}\) on \(R^{-C}\)-large predetermined subsets.

---

# 2. Failure mode A: \(\dim \mathcal V_I\) too large

If

\[
\dim \mathcal V_I
\]

is too large, then \(\mathcal W_G,\mathcal W_{G2}\) may not have polynomial finite-model control.

This is not a D4 failure. It means the routed chart is too coarse.

Route:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Multiscale refinement of }I.
}
\]

Split \(I\) into smaller chart blocks until

\[
\dim \mathcal V_I\le Q^C
\]

and the local Riesz constants are controlled.

This aligns with the existing MultiscaleFoldRouting architecture.

---

# 3. Failure mode B: high leverage in product spaces

Even if \(\mathcal V_I\) is controlled, product spaces such as

\[
\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I
\]

may have near-spikes.

Then functions in \(\mathcal W_G\) can concentrate away from the \(R^{-C}\)-large weight-good set, breaking Remez transfer.

Route:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{ProductLeverageControl}_3
}
\]

or, if it fails,

\[
\boxed{
\text{local product-spike branch.}
}
\]

A product spike is itself strong structure: it says the local source chart can create high leverage cubic packets. That should either be ruled out by Christoffel spread, or routed as a bad finite-model branch.

This is analogous to O2/O3 Christoffel spread, so the same machinery may handle it.

---

# 4. Failure mode C: no \(D^4\)-control

Since

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}
\]

contains \(q^{(4)}\), \(\mathcal W_{G2}\) requires

\[
D^4\mathcal V_I.
\]

If the local atlas only controls up to \(D^2\), then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) is not a stable wall component.

Route options:

1. Upgrade the finite atlas to \(C^4/H^4\)-control.
2. Use the D4-supplied \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) as an independent finite observable with its own Riesz space, not derived from \(q\).
3. If neither works, rank-two analytic closure stalls.

This is a serious but explicit regularity requirement.

---

# 5. Failure mode D: \(I^*\) crosses chart boundaries

The Markov inequality

\[
\|\Gamma_G\|_{H^2(I)}
\le Q^C\|\Gamma_G\|_{L^2(I^*)}
\]

requires \(I^*\) to stay inside a controlled chart.

If \(I^*\) crosses fold/transition boundaries, the Markov bound may fail.

Route:

\[
\boxed{
\text{use smaller }I,\text{ or a transition-compatible enlarged block }I^*.
}
\]

This is routing, not analytic failure.

---

# 6. Failure mode E: finite residual lacks \(H^2/L^2\) completion

If

\[
q=q_X+r
\]

and

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2}(\Gamma_G(q),\mathcal W_G)
\]

is not small, then the finite-model Markov theorem does not apply to the actual wall.

Need:

\[
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}.
\]

Specifically:

\[
\|\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X)\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X)\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}.
\]

If this fails, then the rank-two wall branch requires stronger local completion. This is likely the main analytic completion blocker.

---

# 7. New opportunity: finite-atlas failures can feed Gate–1

Several failure modes imply concentration or high leverage in the finite source chart. Such concentration may itself conflict with Gate–1 or O2/O3 routing.

For example, high product leverage in

\[
\operatorname{Sym}^3\mathcal V_I
\]

means \(q^3\)-type expressions can concentrate sharply. But Gate–1 observes \(q''\), and O2/O3 observes finite polynomial/fold moments. There may be a direct contradiction or at least a stronger routing condition.

So finite-atlas failure is not necessarily fatal. It creates a new structured bad branch:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasDegeneracy}_{2Y}.
}
\]

This branch should be handled by multiscale refinement or by a finite-model nonabsorption theorem.

---

# 8. Updated analytic dependency graph

The analytic side now has:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{Gate1}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}}
}
\]

But \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) itself decomposes into:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{DimensionControl}}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{ProductLeverageControl}_3}
\]

\[
\boxed{
D^4\text{-regularity}}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{ChartEnlargementControl}}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{Remez}_{R^{-C}}}.
\]

This is now a precise analytic checklist.

---

# 9. What to prioritize

The next analytic priority should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}
}
\]

because even perfect finite-atlas Riesz only applies to the finite approximation. The actual wall contains residuals.

Second priority:

\[
\boxed{
D^4\text{-regularity for }\Gamma_{G,2}.
}
\]

Without it, \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) is not in a controlled finite wall space.

Third priority:

\[
\boxed{
ProductLeverageControl_3.
}
\]

This handles both LocalMarkov and weighted transfer.

---

# 10. Current conclusion

The rank-two analytic path remains coherent, but the finite-atlas theorem should be treated as a package with explicit sub-obligations.

The strongest current rank-two route is:

\[
B_Y=0
\]

\[
\Rightarrow
F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\]

\[
\Rightarrow
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}
+
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
\]

\[
\Rightarrow
\text{plain weighted wall mass}
\]

\[
\Rightarrow
\text{scalar O2/O3}.
\]

The local algebra remains the top bottleneck, but the analytic side is now a finite-atlas regularity/completion problem, not a moving-wall problem.

\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}.

This is narrower than the old full singular completion problem. It only controls the two scalar wall functions:

\[
\Gamma_G,\qquad \Gamma_{G,2}.
\]

---

# 1. Define the wall residuals

Let

\[
q=q_X+r,
\]

where \(q_X\) is the finite EF/AFE/chart approximation and \(r\) is the completion residual.

Define

\[
\Gamma_G(q)
=
-6q''+36q-12q^3,
\]

and

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}(q)
=
q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''.
\]

Then the wall residuals are

\[
R_G
:=
\Gamma_G(q)-\Gamma_G(q_X),
\]

\[
R_{G2}
:=
\Gamma_{G,2}(q)-\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X).
\]

The completion theorem needed is:

\[
\boxed{
\|R_G\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|R_{G2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B}
}
\]

on routed rank-two blocks.

That is the exact residual needed for:

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}.
\]

---

# 2. Expand \(R_G\)

We have

\[
\Gamma_G(q)
=
-6q''+36q-12q^3.
\]

So

\[
R_G
=
-6r''+36r-12\bigl((q_X+r)^3-q_X^3\bigr).
\]

Expanding:

\[
R_G
=
-6r''+36r
-36q_X^2r
-36q_Xr^2
-12r^3.
\]

Thus

\[
\boxed{
R_G
=
-6r''
+
(36-36q_X^2)r
-36q_Xr^2
-12r^3.
}
\]

To control \(R_G\) in \(H^2\), sufficient assumptions are:

\[
\|r\|_{H^4(I)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

\[
\|q_X\|_{C^2(I)}\le Q^C,
\]

and product Sobolev estimates on the local chart.

So \(R_G\) requires fourth-order residual control of \(q\), because \(H^2\) is applied to \(r''\).

This is the first important fact:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}\text{ needs residual control up to }H^4.
}
\]

---

# 3. Expand \(R_{G2}\)

Now

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}(q)
=
q^{(4)}-6(1-q^2)q''
=
q^{(4)}-6q''+6q^2q''.
\]

Therefore

\[
R_{G2}
=
r^{(4)}-6r''
+
6\left(q^2q''-q_X^2q_X''\right).
\]

Since

\[
q=q_X+r,
\qquad
q''=q_X''+r'',
\]

we get

\[
q^2q''-q_X^2q_X''
=
(q_X+r)^2(q_X''+r'')-q_X^2q_X''.
\]

Expand:

\[
=
q_X^2r''
+
2q_Xr q_X''
+
2q_Xr r''
+
r^2q_X''
+
r^2r''.
\]

Thus

\[
\boxed{
R_{G2}
=
r^{(4)}-6r''
+
6q_X^2r''
+
12q_Xr q_X''
+
12q_Xr r''
+
6r^2q_X''
+
6r^2r''.
}
\]

To control \(R_{G2}\) in \(L^2\), sufficient assumptions are:

\[
\|r^{(4)}\|_{L^2(I)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

\[
\|r''\|_{L^2(I)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

\[
\|r\|_{L^\infty(I)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

with polynomial bounds on \(q_X,q_X''\).

Again, this is an \(H^4\)-level residual requirement.

---

# 4. Wall completion is \(H^4\), not full order-nine completion

This is still narrower than the old singular \(K_9\) completion because it controls a specific pair of scalar differential expressions:

\[
\Gamma_G(q),\qquad \Gamma_{G,2}(q).
\]

But it is not cheap: because \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) contains \(q^{(4)}\), the finite approximation must control the fourth derivative of the residual.

So the wall completion should be named:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}.
}
\]

Statement:

For every routed rank-two block \(I\),

\[
q=q_X+r
\]

with

\[
\|r\|_{H^4(I)}\le Q^{-B},
\]

and polynomial \(C^4\)-bounds for \(q_X\). Then

\[
\|R_G\|_{H^2(I)}
+
\|R_{G2}\|_{L^2(I)}
\le Q^{-B'}.
\]

This feeds the finite-atlas Markov/coercivity proof.

---

# 5. Possible obstruction: tails may not be \(H^4\)-small

The dangerous point is the tail.

If the EF/AFE truncation only controls

\[
r
\]

or

\[
r''
\]

in \(L^2\), but not

\[
r^{(4)},
\]

then \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) completion fails.

This would not necessarily kill rank two, but it changes the proof.

Possible responses:

## 5.1 Include \(q^{(4)}\) in the finite observable

Choose \(q_X\) so that \(q^{(4)}\) is also approximated by the finite chart.

That means the finite atlas must be built to order \(4\).

## 5.2 Use weak formulation

Instead of controlling \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) in pointwise/L² form, test it against smooth local functions and integrate by parts to move derivatives off \(r^{(4)}\). This may reduce residual requirements.

But D4/O2O3 wall uses pointwise or \(L^2\)-type wall energy, so weak control may not be enough.

## 5.3 Use a smoothed wall

Define the D4 wall after applying a smoothing operator that damps high derivatives. But then one must prove D4 still sees the smoothed wall.

This is nontrivial.

The clean path is order-four finite completion.

---

# 6. Connection to \(B_2\) and gamma background

The current canonical zeta-side tooling already treats

\[
B_2
\]

as a coherent gamma/background curvature term. For rank two, \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) involves \(q^{(4)}\), so gamma/background fourth derivative terms must be correctly included in \(q_X\), not left in the residual.

Thus the finite model should include:

\[
B_2,\quad B_2',\quad B_2'',\quad \text{up to the derivatives needed for }q^{(4)}.
\]

If \(q''=B_2+\cdots\), then

\[
q^{(4)}=(q'')''
\]

requires second derivatives of the \(q''\)-model. So the finite atlas for \(q''\) must have two more local derivatives.

This is manageable but must be explicit.

---

# 7. Revised analytic stack

The analytic side becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{Gate1}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}}
}
\]

The completion theorem must come before Riesz/Coercivity, because those are applied to the finite chart model plus residual.

---

# 8. The finite atlas must be order-four

Previously we could say \(\mathcal V_I\) is a local chart for \(q\). Now we should be more precise.

Either:

## Option A

\(\mathcal V_I\) is a chart for \(q\) with controlled derivatives up to \(4\).

Then

\[
D^4\mathcal V_I
\]

is available for \(\Gamma_{G,2}\).

## Option B

\(\mathcal U_I\) is a chart for \(q''\).

Then

\[
\Gamma_G
=
-6q''+36q-12q^3
\]

also needs \(q\), not just \(q''\), unless \(q\) is recovered by integration with controlled constants.

Option A is cleaner: chart \(q\) to order four.

But in the zeta setup, \(q''\) has been the main observable. If \(q\) itself is less canonical, define the finite chart in terms of \(q''\) plus two integrations with boundary normalization. Need to be careful.

This is another finite atlas design decision.

---

# 9. Potential simplification: express \(\Gamma_G\) using \(q''\) and \(q\), but Gate–1 already observes \(q''\)

If \(q\) is bounded and low-complexity independently, then the cubic term is manageable.

The actual source \(q\) should be a normalized phase/log-amplitude variable, not an arbitrary integral of \(q''\). So \(\mathcal V_I\) likely exists naturally.

But this should be recorded as an assumption:

\[
\boxed{
q\text{ and }q''\text{ belong to the same controlled finite atlas to order four.}
}
\]

This gives \(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\) finite model control.

---

# 10. Current conclusion

The rank-two route is still coherent, but the analytic side now has a clear order-four completion requirement.

The next precise theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}:
\quad
\operatorname{dist}_{H^2\times L^2}
\left(
(\Gamma_G(q),\Gamma_{G,2}(q)),
(\Gamma_G(q_X),\Gamma_{G,2}(q_X))
\right)
\le Q^{-B}.
}
\]

If this closes, the rest of the rank-two analytic package is finite-atlas Riesz plus coercivity.

The local algebraic bottleneck remains the bounded-row certificate for \(B_Y=0\), but analytically the next real issue is fourth-order wall completion.

## Assistant (2026-04-28 14:48:06)

_Thought for 7s_

I’ll keep the existing convention:

\[
\rho_Y=\text{lower }Y12\text{ graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

Critical correction retained: the honest rank-two D4 wall is only

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.
\]

No jet wall components are available unless a separate derivative-D4-channel theorem is proved. 

## Agent 6 — \( \mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y} \) and \( \mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}} \)

Assume:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

Then D4 gives

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

On coefficient-good charts,

\[
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right).
\]

The task is to route enough mass to coefficient-good charts and transfer unweighted wall mass into the weighted O2/O3 wall.

---

# 1. Classify \(H\)

There are three cases.

## Case A — \(H\) is a coefficient or normalization denominator

Then \(H\) belongs in the D4 chart-good condition:

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

This is the preferred route. \(H\)-smallness means the chosen D4 coordinate chart is bad, not that the wall failed.

## Case B — \(H\) is a finite low-complexity amplitude

Then require an \(H\)-nonabsorption/sublevel theorem:

\[
\int_{\{|H|<\varepsilon\}}
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu
\le
Q^C\varepsilon^\theta
\int
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)d\nu.
\]

## Case C — \(H\) is locally adjustable/free

Then this D4 normalization can suppress the wall locally. The rank-two route fails in this chart unless \(H\) is renormalized away, another D4 chart is used, or a different carrier is chosen.

---

# 2. \( \mathrm{D4CoeffGood}_{2Y} \)

Define the coefficient-good condition:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGood}_{2Y}
}
\]

by

\[
|H|\ge Q^{-A},
\]

\[
|c_7|,\ |c_7^{-1}|,\ |\alpha_9|,\ |\alpha_9^{-1}|,\ |\beta_9|\le Q^A.
\]

If row triangularization changes

\[
\beta_9\mapsto \widetilde\beta_9,
\]

then replace the last condition by

\[
|\widetilde\beta_9|\le Q^A.
\]

On this set,

\[
\|F_{2Y}\|^2
\asymp_{Q^A}
|H|^2W_2,
\]

where

\[
W_2:=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2.
\]

---

# 3. \( \mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y} \)

The routing theorem should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}.
}
\]

It states that a polynomial portion of Gate–1/plain-wall mass lies on D4-good charts:

\[
\sum_{\Omega\subset \mathcal C_{\D4}}
\int_\Omega W_2\,d\nu
\ge
Q^{-A}
\sum_{\Omega\subset \mathcal C}
\int_\Omega W_2\,d\nu,
\]

where \(\mathcal C_{\D4}\subseteq \mathcal C\) is the D4 coefficient-good subfamily.

If this fails, the route enters an \(H\)-degenerate or coefficient-degenerate branch:

1. use an alternate D4 chart;
2. renormalize \(H\);
3. prove \(H\)-sublevel/nonabsorption if \(H\) is finite-model;
4. or accept a no-go for this carrier normalization.

---

# 4. Non-circular weighted transfer

Define

\[
\Omega_{\sm}:=N_{\sm}|H|^2.
\]

For each routed block \(I\), define the predetermined good-weight set

\[
E_I:=\{m\in I:\Omega_{\sm}(m)\ge Q^{-A}\}.
\]

This set may depend on chart, coefficient, and smooth cutoff data. It must **not** depend on

\[
\Gamma_G,\quad \Gamma_{G,2},\quad W_2,
\]

nor on extracted atoms or live residuals.

The needed size condition is

\[
\boxed{
|E_I|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
}
\]

The \(R^{-C}\) threshold is important: \(Q^{-C}|I|\) can be too small for degree-\(R\) Remez losses.

---

# 5. Vector wall Remez

Assume \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) gives scalar Remez for both components. Then for any predetermined

\[
E\subset I,\qquad |E|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

we have

\[
\boxed{
\int_E W_2\,d\nu
\ge
Q^{-C}
\int_I W_2\,d\nu.
}
\]

Proof: whichever component \(\Gamma_G\) or \(\Gamma_{G,2}\) carries at least half the \(L^2(I)\)-mass transfers by scalar Remez.

---

# 6. \( \mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}} \)

The theorem is:

Assume on selected routed blocks \(I\):

\[
\int_I W_2\,d\nu\ge Q^{-A}|I|,
\]

\[
E_I=\{\Omega_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}\},
\qquad
|E_I|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

and vector wall Remez holds. Then

\[
\int_I \Omega_{\sm} W_2\,d\nu
\ge
Q^{-A'}|I|.
\]

Indeed,

\[
\int_I\Omega_{\sm}W_2
\ge
\int_{E_I}\Omega_{\sm}W_2
\ge
Q^{-A}\int_{E_I}W_2
\ge
Q^{-A-C}\int_IW_2.
\]

Summing over selected blocks yields

\[
\boxed{
\int
N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)d\nu
\ge
Q^{-A'}.
}
\]

---

# 7. Scalar component selection

From weighted wall mass,

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\right)d\nu
\ge Q^{-A},
\]

select one fixed scalar component at the smooth aggregate stage:

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

such that

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2\,d\nu
\ge Q^{-A'}.
\]

Do not choose \(G_\star\) atomwise after extraction. If blockwise selection is needed, partition blocks by dominant component before hard extraction and keep one polynomial-mass class.

O2/O3 then uses

\[
\boxed{
W_\star=N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
}
\]

The branch bandwidth is

\[
\boxed{
d_0^{(2)}
=
d_N+2d_H+2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}).
}
\]

Need

\[
d_0^{(2)}\le d_{\max},
\]

or else increase \(d_{\max}\) to the required fixed value.

---

# 8. Failure routing

## \(H\)-small wall mass

If wall mass lies where \(|H|\ll Q^{-A}\), classify \(H\):

- denominator: chart/routing failure;
- finite amplitude: prove \(H\)-nonabsorption;
- local/free: current normalization fails.

## \(N_{\sm}\) cuts away wall mass

Then the extraction cutoff is misdesigned. Adjust \(N_{\sm}\) or the routed good set.

## \(E_I\) not \(R^{-C}\)-large

Weighted transfer fails. Need new routing or a larger predetermined good-weight set.

## Vector Remez fails

Then \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) fails; this is a finite-atlas degeneracy.

## Atomwise scalar selection

Invalid. It creates a moving selector and can break bounded charge.

---

# 9. Final package

The correct bridge to O2/O3 is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}}.
}
\]

It outputs a fixed scalar wall

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

such that scalar O2/O3 applies to

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
\]

This preserves the correction: no derivative wall components enter the D4/O2O3 wall unless derivative D4 channels are separately proven.

\[
\rho_Y=\text{lower }Y12\text{ graph slope},\qquad
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},\qquad
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

## Agent 6 continued attack — weighted transfer as a three-set routing problem

The corrected rank-two route now uses the honest plain wall

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\},
\]

not the jet wall. After \(B_Y=0\), D4 gives

\[
F_{2Y}
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\asymp
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\]

on D4 coefficient-good charts.

The weighted transfer layer needs to move from unweighted wall mass

\[
W_2:=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2
\]

to weighted wall mass

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2.
\]

The right formulation is a **three-set routing problem**.

---

# 1. Three sets must overlap

On each routed block \(I\), define:

\[
E_{\D4}(I)
=
\{m\in I:\mathrm{D4CoeffGood}_{2Y}(m)\},
\]

\[
E_{\sm}(I)
=
\{m\in I:N_{\sm}(m)\ge Q^{-A}\},
\]

and

\[
E_H(I)
=
\{m\in I:|H(m)|\ge Q^{-A}\}.
\]

If \(H\) is included in D4CoeffGood, then \(E_H\) is part of \(E_{\D4}\).

The effective good-weight set is

\[
E_I
=
E_{\D4}(I)\cap E_{\sm}(I).
\]

Weighted transfer needs

\[
\boxed{
|E_I|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
}
\]

Then Remez transfers wall mass:

\[
\int_{E_I}W_2\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2.
\]

And since on \(E_I\),

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2\ge Q^{-A},
\]

we get

\[
\int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2
\ge
Q^{-A-C}\int_I W_2.
\]

So the transfer theorem reduces to proving:

\[
\boxed{
E_{\D4}\cap E_{\sm}
\text{ is predetermined and }R^{-C}\text{-large.}
}
\]

---

# 2. Predetermined means independent of \(W_2\)

The set

\[
E_I=E_{\D4}\cap E_{\sm}
\]

may depend on:

\[
H,\ c_7,\alpha_9,\beta_9,\ N_{\sm},\ \text{chart data},\ \text{atlas routing}.
\]

It must not depend on:

\[
\Gamma_G,\quad \Gamma_{G,2},\quad W_2,
\]

nor on atom-level extraction choices.

This is critical. If \(E_I\) is chosen after seeing where \(W_2\) is large, the Remez step is circular.

Thus, the correct routing theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{PredeterminedGoodSet}_{2Y}:
\quad
E_I=\{ \mathrm{D4CoeffGood}\}\cap\{N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}\}
\text{ is fixed before wall extraction and }|E_I|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
}
\]

---

# 3. D4CoeffGoodRouting should be block-local

Previously, I stated D4CoeffGoodRouting as “a polynomial portion of wall mass lies on D4-good charts.” That still depends on wall mass and is slightly late.

A cleaner, non-circular version is:

For every selected routed block \(I\),

\[
\boxed{
|E_{\D4}(I)|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
}
\]

Then Remez ensures wall mass cannot avoid \(E_{\D4}\).

So instead of asking:

\[
\int_{E_{\D4}} W_2 \ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2,
\]

we ask for the deterministic size condition

\[
|E_{\D4}|\ge R^{-C}|I|
\]

and let Remez prove the wall-mass version.

This is stronger and cleaner.

---

# 4. Same for \(N_{\sm}\)

Similarly, for the smooth weight:

\[
E_{\sm}(I)=\{N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}\}.
\]

Require

\[
|E_{\sm}(I)|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

But we need the intersection

\[
E_{\D4}\cap E_{\sm}
\]

to be \(R^{-C}\)-large, not each separately. Two \(R^{-C}\)-large sets can be disjoint.

So the correct hypothesis is directly:

\[
\boxed{
|E_{\D4}(I)\cap E_{\sm}(I)|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
}
\]

This should be enforced by constructing \(N_{\sm}\) after the D4 chart routing, or by aligning both with the same routed good block.

---

# 5. Revised weighted transfer theorem

The clean theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}}.
}
\]

For every selected routed block \(I\), assume:

1. \(W_2\) lies in the finite Remez class generated by \(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\);

2. the predetermined set

\[
E_I=
\{m\in I:\mathrm{D4CoeffGood}_{2Y}(m),\ N_{\sm}(m)\ge Q^{-A}\}
\]

satisfies

\[
|E_I|\ge R^{-C}|I|;
\]

3. vector Remez holds:

\[
\int_E W_2\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2
\quad
\text{for every }E\subset I,\ |E|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

Then

\[
\int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2
\ge Q^{-C'}\int_I W_2.
\]

This is the non-circular transfer.

---

# 6. How to guarantee \(E_I\) is large

There are three strategies.

## Strategy A — build \(N_{\sm}\) on D4-good charts

Define the smooth cutoff \(N_{\sm}\) so that on the selected D4-good core,

\[
N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Then

\[
E_I=E_{\D4,\mathrm{core}}(I)
\]

and size follows from D4 chart routing.

This is the cleanest.

## Strategy B — select blocks where both are good

During routing, keep only blocks satisfying

\[
|E_{\D4}\cap E_{\sm}|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

Show these blocks retain polynomial Gate–1 mass.

This is slightly weaker but still safe.

## Strategy C — prove nonavoidance between \(E_{\D4}\) and \(E_{\sm}\)

If both sets are defined independently, prove their overlap is large. This is unnecessary if we can use A or B.

Preferred: **Strategy A**.

---

# 7. Interaction with scalar component selection

After weighted transfer,

\[
\int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2\ge Q^{-A}\int_I W_2,
\]

summing over blocks gives

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2\right)
\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

Now select

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

globally or on a pre-extraction block class.

This selection may depend on the **weighted aggregate integrals**

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2|\Gamma_G|^2,
\qquad
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2.
\]

That is allowed because it occurs at the smooth aggregate stage.

It may not depend on atom-level local values.

---

# 8. Failure modes are now sharply separated

## 8.1 \(E_I\) too small

This is a routing/cutoff failure. Fix D4 chart selection or \(N_{\sm}\).

## 8.2 Remez fails for \(W_2\)

This is finite-atlas failure: \(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\) lack Remez control.

## 8.3 \(H\)-degenerate blocks dominate before \(E_I\) selection

This is D4CoeffGoodRouting failure. Use alternate chart, renormalization, or \(H\)-nonabsorption.

## 8.4 Weighted mass exists but scalar component selection is atomwise

Invalid. Redo selection at aggregate/block-class level.

---

# 9. Current revised transfer stack

The transfer layer should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodCore}_{2Y}
}
\]

constructs a predetermined \(R^{-C}\)-large subset per block where

\[
|H|,\ c_7,\alpha_9,\beta_9
\]

are conditioned.

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NSmoothAligned}_{2Y}
}
\]

ensures \(N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}\) on the same core.

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{VectorRemez}_{W_2}
}
\]

transfers wall mass to that core.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}^{R,\mathrm{nc}}
}
\]

is immediate.

This is more precise than the previous formulation.

---

# 10. Current conclusion

The weighted transfer is sound if we strengthen the routing hypothesis from “wall mass lies on D4-good charts” to:

\[
\boxed{
\text{each selected block has a predetermined }R^{-C}\text{-large D4/smooth-good core.}
}
\]

Then finite-atlas Remez transfers \(W_2\)-mass into that core.

This removes the last circularity in the O2/O3 transfer layer.

F_{2Y}\asymp H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})

on D4-good charts, and the honest wall is only

\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.

The transfer to O2/O3 should not say vaguely that “wall mass is on good charts.” It should be formulated through a **predetermined good core** inside each block.

---

# 1. Predetermined good core

For each selected routed block \(I\), define

\[
E_I^{\core}
\subset I
\]

before wall extraction.

It may depend on:

\[
H,\quad c_7,\quad \alpha_9,\quad \beta_9,\quad N_{\sm},\quad \text{chart/atlas data}.
\]

It must not depend on:

\[
\Gamma_G,\quad \Gamma_{G,2},\quad W_2,\quad \text{hard-extracted atoms}.
\]

The required properties are:

\[
\boxed{
|E_I^{\core}|\ge R^{-C}|I|;
}
\]

and on \(E_I^{\core}\),

\[
\boxed{
|H|\ge Q^{-A},\quad
|c_7|,|c_7^{-1}|,|\alpha_9|,|\alpha_9^{-1}|,|\beta_9|\le Q^A,
\quad
N_{\sm}\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

If rows are triangularized, replace \(\beta_9\) by \(\widetilde\beta_9\).

This single object absorbs:

\[
\mathrm{D4CoeffGoodRouting}_{2Y}
\]

and

\[
N_{\sm}\text{-compatibility}
\]

into one non-circular condition.

---

# 2. Core transfer by vector Remez

Let

\[
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2.
\]

Assume the finite-atlas Remez theorem:

\[
|E|\ge R^{-C}|I|
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\int_E W_2
\ge Q^{-C}\int_I W_2.
\]

Apply this to the predetermined set \(E_I^{\core}\). Then

\[
\int_{E_I^{\core}}W_2
\ge Q^{-C}\int_IW_2.
\]

Since on \(E_I^{\core}\),

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2\ge Q^{-A},
\]

we get

\[
\int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2
\ge
\int_{E_I^{\core}}N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2
\ge
Q^{-A-C}\int_IW_2.
\]

Thus the transfer is automatic.

This is the clean theorem:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{CoreWeightedTransfer}_{2Y}.
}
\]

---

# 3. What needs to be proved before transfer

The transfer theorem requires three upstream facts.

## 3.1 Core existence

For a polynomial-mass routed block family, each block has a predetermined core

\[
E_I^{\core}
\]

of size

\[
R^{-C}|I|.
\]

This is an atlas/coefficient routing theorem.

## 3.2 Finite-atlas Remez for \(W_2\)

Need Remez for

\[
\Gamma_G
\]

and

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}.
\]

Since

\[
W_2
\]

is a two-component norm, scalar Remez for each component suffices.

## 3.3 Unweighted wall lower

Need

\[
\int_IW_2
\]

large on selected blocks. This comes from:

\[
\mathrm{LocalMarkov}_{\Gamma_G}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}.
\]

These three are independent. That independence is the key non-circularity.

---

# 4. Core existence cases

## Case A: \(H,c_7,\alpha_9,\beta_9,N_{\sm}\) are all chart-controlled

Then define the selected block family so each block is already coefficient-good except for a small boundary layer. Let

\[
E_I^{\core}
\]

be the interior coefficient-good subblock. This should be the default.

## Case B: \(H\) is a finite amplitude with zeros

Then core existence requires sublevel control for \(H\):

\[
|\{|H|\ge Q^{-A}\}\cap I|\ge R^{-C}|I|.
\]

This is an \(H\)-Remez/nonabsorption theorem.

## Case C: \(N_{\sm}\) is too narrow

Then redefine \(N_{\sm}\). Since \(N_{\sm}\) is proof-chosen, it should not destroy the core unless a previous extraction condition forces it.

## Case D: no core exists

Then rank-two route cannot use this D4 chart on that block. Reroute or classify as D4-coefficient-degenerate.

---

# 5. Scalar O2/O3 after core transfer

Once

\[
\sum_I\int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2
\ge Q^{-A},
\]

select one fixed component

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}
\]

by aggregate pigeonhole:

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2\ge Q^{-A'}.
\]

Then run scalar O2/O3 with

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2.
\]

The scalar selection may be over a pre-extraction block class, but it cannot be atomwise.

---

# 6. Relation to \(d_0\)

The selected scalar wall has bandwidth

\[
d_0(G_\star)=d_N+2d_H+2d_{G_\star}.
\]

Thus the rank-two plain wall requires

\[
\boxed{
d_0^{(2)}
=
d_N+2d_H+2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}})
\le d_{\max}.
}
\]

No derivative components enter \(d_0\).

This is another benefit of respecting the honest plain wall.

---

# 7. Current complete transfer theorem

The final transfer theorem should be:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}.
}
\]

Assume a routed block family \(\mathcal B\) with predetermined cores \(E_I^{\core}\subset I\) satisfying:

\[
|E_I^{\core}|\ge R^{-C}|I|,
\]

\[
N_{\sm}|H|^2\ge Q^{-A}
\quad\text{on }E_I^{\core}.
\]

Assume \(\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}\) gives vector Remez for

\[
W_2=|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2.
\]

Then

\[
\sum_{I\in\mathcal B}
\int_I N_{\sm}|H|^2W_2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\sum_{I\in\mathcal B}
\int_I W_2.
\]

If the right side is polynomial by Gate–1/coercivity, O2/O3 receives weighted wall mass.

---

# 8. Remaining dependency graph

Rank-two now depends on:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{BoundedDualRows}_{2Y}
}
\]

for \(B_Y=0\);

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{D4CoeffCoreRouting}_{2Y}
}
\]

for predetermined \(E_I^{\core}\);

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{2Y,\wall}^{(4)}
+
\mathrm{FiniteAtlasRiesz}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{RankTwoCoercivity}
+
\mathrm{Gate1}
}
\]

for unweighted wall mass;

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{CoreWeightedPlainWallTransfer}_{2Y}
}
\]

for weighted wall mass;

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{ScalarO2/O3}(G_\star)
}
\]

for the final determinant survival.

This is now internally consistent and non-circular.

---

\[

are all good?

\boxed{

\ge

Q^{-A'}\int_IW_2.

d_0(G_\star)=d_N+2d_H+2d_{G_\star}\le d_{\max}.

For rank two,

\[
G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\}.

d_0^{(2)}
=

are not part of the O2/O3 wall unless derivative D4 channels are proved.

\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\mu_{\sub})}

\ge Q^{-A}.

with

R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.

d_0\le d_{\max}.

\[
G_\star

the smooth aggregate wall mass selects.

---

The rank-two wall lower after transfer is:

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2
\left(
|\Gamma_G|^2+|\Gamma_{G,2}|^2

\ge Q^{-A}.

G_\star\in\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\},

\[
\int N_{\sm}|H|^2|G_\star|^2d\nu
\ge Q^{-A'}.
\]

Then O2/O3 only needs to preserve this scalar branch weight.

Thus the whole vector nature of rank two is consumed before O2/O3. O2/O3 is scalar.

=

\ge

for

G=\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}.

One class has polynomial total weighted wall mass.

Choose that class and that component before hard extraction.

This remains valid because:

1. only two classes exist;
2. the component is fixed within the retained class;
3. the selection uses smooth aggregate block integrals, not atomwise wall values.

---

exists, do not project

before O2/O3.

\to

\to
\text{O2/O3}
\to
\text{vector amplitude Schur}
\to
\text{block contradiction}.

This preserves bounded branch charge.

Projected wall weights are allowed only if the projection has proven bounded branch complexity, which should not be assumed.

---

\mathrm{RowNormalForm}_{2Y}
+
\mathrm{RowExactness}_{2Y}

B_Y=0.

Then final contradiction proceeds through the ordinary \(Y12\) upper/lower comparison, with vector amplitude Schur inserted after O2/O3 if needed.

# 8. Remaining finite degree audit

The key O2/O3 bookkeeping question is:

\[
\boxed{
d_N+2d_H+2\max(d_{\Gamma_G},d_{\Gamma_{G,2}})
\le d_{\max}?
}

\[
d_{\Gamma_G},

d_{\Gamma_{G,2}}.

\[

and

\[

q,\quad q'',\quad q^3,\quad q^{(4)},\quad q^2q''.
\]

Under the finite-atlas product convention, this is fixed-degree algebra. The degree should be \(O(1)\), but the exact number depends on branch-charge conventions.

This audit must be done before finalizing \(d_{\max}=14\).

---

If

\[
d_0^{(2)}>14
\]

but still

with \(R^C\)-conditioning.

---

}
\]

\[
